title.en: Volume 22: Treatise on Rites and Music
title.raw: 卷二十二 禮樂志 Volume 22: Treatise on Rites and Music

sentences.zh: 六經之道同歸，而禮樂之用為急。

translations.literal: The ways of the Six Classics return to the same end, but the practical use of rites and music is most urgent.
translations.idiomatic: All six classics aim at the same ultimate truth, but in practice nothing is more pressing than ritual and music.

sentences.zh: 治身者斯須忘禮，則暴嫚入之矣；

translations.literal: If one who governs himself forgets ritual even for a moment, then violence and arrogance enter him.
translations.idiomatic: If a person neglects ritual even briefly, brutality and contempt quickly take hold.

sentences.zh: 為國者一朝失禮，則荒亂及之矣。

translations.literal: If one who rules a state loses ritual for a single morning, then desolation and disorder reach it.
translations.idiomatic: If a ruler lets ritual lapse even for a day, chaos and ruin soon follow.

sentences.zh: 人函天地陰陽之氣，有喜怒哀樂之情。

translations.literal: Human beings contain the qi of Heaven and Earth, of yin and yang, and possess the feelings of joy, anger, sorrow, and happiness.
translations.idiomatic: People embody the forces of heaven and earth, yin and yang, and naturally experience joy, anger, grief, and delight.

sentences.zh: 天稟其性而不能節也，聖人能為之節而不能絕也，故象天地而制禮樂，所以通神明，立人倫，正情性，節萬事者也。

translations.literal: Heaven endows their nature but cannot regulate it; sages can regulate it but cannot cut it off. Therefore, modeling Heaven and Earth, they established rites and music, by which to communicate with spirits and bright powers, establish human relations, correct emotions and nature, and regulate the myriad affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven gives people their nature but does not curb it; sages can discipline it, though they cannot erase it. So, taking heaven and earth as their model, they created ritual and music to align with the spiritual order, ground human relationships, rectify human feelings, and bring measure to all things.

sentences.zh: 人性有男女之情，妒忌之別，為制婚姻之禮；

translations.literal: Human nature has the feelings between man and woman and the distinctions of jealousy; for this, the rites of marriage were instituted.
translations.idiomatic: Because men and women are drawn to one another and jealousy arises among them, marriage rites were established.

sentences.zh: 有交接長幼之序，為制鄉飲之禮；

translations.literal: There is an order of interaction between elders and juniors; for this, the rites of village drinking were instituted.
translations.idiomatic: Because social life requires clear order between old and young, the village-drinking rites were created.

sentences.zh: 有哀死思遠之情，為制喪祭之禮；

translations.literal: There are feelings of grieving the dead and thinking of those far away; for this, the rites of mourning and sacrifice were instituted.
translations.idiomatic: Because people grieve the dead and remember distant ancestors, mourning and sacrificial rites were established.

sentences.zh: 有尊尊敬上之心，為制朝覲之禮。

translations.literal: There is a heart that honors the honored and respects those above; for this, the rites of court audience were instituted.
translations.idiomatic: Because people are moved to honor rank and respect superiors, rites of court audience were instituted.

sentences.zh: 哀有哭踊之節，樂有歌舞之容，正人足以副其誠，邪人足以防其失。

translations.literal: In mourning there are regulated measures for weeping and leaping; in joy there are forms for song and dance. For upright people these suffice to match their sincerity; for depraved people these suffice to prevent their transgressions.
translations.idiomatic: Grief has set forms for lamentation, and joy has proper forms for song and dance. Such forms let the upright fully express sincerity, and they restrain the wayward from excess.

sentences.zh: 故婚姻之禮廢，則夫婦之道苦，而淫辟之罪多；

translations.literal: Therefore, if the rites of marriage are abolished, then the way of husband and wife is afflicted, and crimes of licentiousness and deviancy become many.
translations.idiomatic: So when marriage rites break down, relations between husband and wife sour, and sexual misconduct multiplies.

sentences.zh: 鄉飲之禮廢，則長幼之序亂，而爭鬥之獄蕃；

translations.literal: If the rites of village drinking are abolished, then the order of elder and younger is disordered, and lawsuits over quarrels and fighting become numerous.
translations.idiomatic: If village rites collapse, the hierarchy between old and young is thrown into confusion, and disputes and brawling cases proliferate.

sentences.zh: 喪祭之禮廢，則骨肉之恩薄，而背死忘先者眾；

translations.literal: If the rites of mourning and sacrifice are abolished, then affection among kin of flesh and bone grows thin, and those who turn their backs on the dead and forget their forebears are many.
translations.idiomatic: If mourning and ancestral rites are neglected, family bonds weaken, and many people disregard the dead and forget their ancestors.

sentences.zh: 朝聘之禮廢，則君臣之位失，而侵陵之漸起。

translations.literal: If the rites of court audience and diplomatic visits are abolished, then the positions of ruler and minister are lost, and the tendency toward encroachment and aggression begins to arise.
translations.idiomatic: If court and diplomatic rites fall away, the proper order between ruler and minister breaks down, and mutual encroachment starts to grow.

sentences.zh: 故孔子曰：「安上治民，莫殒於禮；

translations.literal: Therefore Confucius said: 'To secure those above and govern the people, nothing surpasses ritual;
translations.idiomatic: Thus Confucius said, 'To stabilize superiors and govern the people, nothing is better than ritual;'

sentences.zh: 移風易俗，莫殒於樂。」

translations.literal: To transform customs and alter habits, nothing surpasses music.
translations.idiomatic: Nothing reshapes social custom more effectively than music.

sentences.zh: 禮節民心，樂和民聲，政以行之，刑以防之。

translations.literal: Ritual disciplines the people's hearts; music harmonizes the people's voices; governance puts this into practice; punishments prevent transgression.
translations.idiomatic: Ritual orders people's minds, music harmonizes expression, governance carries it out, and punishment checks violations.

sentences.zh: 禮樂政刑四達而不誖，則王道備矣。

translations.literal: When ritual, music, governance, and punishment all reach everywhere without contradiction, then the kingly way is complete.
translations.idiomatic: When rites, music, policy, and law work in concert across the realm, the kingly way is complete.

sentences.zh: 樂以治內而為同，禮以修外而為異；

translations.literal: Music governs what is inward and creates sameness; ritual cultivates what is outward and creates distinction.
translations.idiomatic: Music unifies inwardly, while ritual differentiates outwardly.

sentences.zh: 同則和親，異則畏敬；

translations.literal: Sameness brings harmonious closeness; distinction brings awe and respect.
translations.idiomatic: Unity fosters closeness, while distinction fosters reverence.

sentences.zh: 和親則無怨，畏敬則不爭。

translations.literal: Where there is harmonious closeness, there is no resentment; where there is awe and respect, there is no contention.
translations.idiomatic: Affection prevents bitterness, and reverence prevents strife.

sentences.zh: 揖讓而天下治者，禮樂之謂也。

translations.literal: That all under heaven is governed through bowing and yielding is what is meant by ritual and music.
translations.idiomatic: When the world is governed through courtesy and deference, that is the work of ritual and music.

sentences.zh: 二者並行，合為一體。

translations.literal: The two proceed together and combine into one body.
translations.idiomatic: These two operate together as a single system.

sentences.zh: 畏敬之意難見，則著之於享獻辭受，登降跪拜；

translations.literal: The meaning of awe and respect is hard to see, so it is made manifest in offering and receiving, ascending and descending, kneeling and bowing.
translations.idiomatic: Because reverence is inward and hard to perceive, it is expressed through ceremony: offering and receiving, rising and descending, kneeling and bowing.

sentences.zh: 和親之說難形，則發之於詩歌詠言，鐘石筦弦。

translations.literal: The teaching of harmonious closeness is hard to give form to, so it is expressed in poems and songs, bells, stone chimes, pipes, and strings.
translations.idiomatic: Because harmony and affection are hard to depict directly, they are conveyed through verse, song, and the sounds of bells, chimes, winds, and strings.

sentences.zh: 蓋嘉其敬意而不及其財賄，美其歡心而不流其聲音。

translations.literal: They prized reverent intention rather than material gifts, and valued joyful hearts rather than the mere overflow of sound.
translations.idiomatic: What mattered was sincere reverence over wealth, and genuine joy over sheer sonic display.

sentences.zh: 故孔子曰：「

translations.literal: Therefore Confucius said:
translations.idiomatic: Thus Confucius remarked:

sentences.zh: 禮云禮云，玉帛云乎哉？

translations.literal: 'When one says ritual, ritual, is it merely jade and silk?'
translations.idiomatic: 'When we speak of ritual, can it be reduced to jade and silk offerings?'

sentences.zh: 樂云樂云，鐘鼓云乎哉？」

translations.literal: 'When one says music, music, is it merely bells and drums?'
translations.idiomatic: 'When we speak of music, is it only bells and drums?'

sentences.zh: 此禮樂之本也。

translations.literal: This is the root of ritual and music.
translations.idiomatic: This is the fundamental principle of ritual and music.

sentences.zh: 故曰：「知禮樂之情者能作，識禮樂之文者能述；

translations.literal: Therefore it is said: 'Those who understand the substance of ritual and music can create them; those who know the forms of ritual and music can transmit them.'
translations.idiomatic: Hence the saying: those who grasp the inner substance of rites and music can originate them, while those who master their forms can pass them on.

sentences.zh: 作者之謂聖，述者之謂明。

translations.literal: Those who create are called sage; those who transmit are called discerning.
translations.idiomatic: Creators are called sages, and transmitters are called those of clear understanding.

sentences.zh: 明聖者，述作之謂也。」

translations.literal: To be discerning and sage is to be able both to transmit and to create.'
translations.idiomatic: True sage discernment lies in both transmission and creation.

sentences.zh: 王者必因前王之禮，順時施宜，有所損益，即民之心，稍稍制作，至太平而大備。

translations.literal: A king must rely on the rites of former kings, apply what suits the times, make reductions and additions, accord with the people's hearts, and gradually establish institutions, until in great peace they are fully complete.
translations.idiomatic: A ruler should inherit earlier rites, adapt them to present conditions, and adjust them by popular sentiment, building institutions step by step until they reach full form in an age of peace.

sentences.zh: 周監於二代，禮文尤具，事為之制，曲為之防，故稱禮經三百，威儀三千。

translations.literal: Zhou took the two earlier dynasties as a mirror; its ritual culture was especially complete. It set regulations for affairs and safeguards for every detail; thus people spoke of three hundred canons of rites and three thousand rules of deportment.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhou, learning from the prior two dynasties, developed an exceptionally complete ritual order, with detailed regulations for institutions and conduct, hence the saying of 'three hundred rites' and 'three thousand observances.'

sentences.zh: 於是教化浹洽，民用和睦，災害不生，禍亂不作，囹圄空虛，四十餘年。

translations.literal: Thus moral education became pervasive, the people lived in harmony, disasters did not arise, chaos did not occur, and prisons were empty for more than forty years.
translations.idiomatic: As a result, instruction spread widely, society was harmonious, calamities and disorder were rare, and prisons stood largely empty for over forty years.

sentences.zh: 孔子美之曰：「郁郁乎文哉！

translations.literal: Confucius praised it, saying: 'How rich and refined is this culture!
translations.idiomatic: Confucius admired it: 'How flourishing and refined this culture is!

sentences.zh: 吾從周。」

translations.literal: I follow Zhou.'
translations.idiomatic: I take Zhou as my model.'

sentences.zh: 及其衰也，諸侯踰越法度，惡禮制之害己，去其篇籍。

translations.literal: When it declined, the feudal lords overstepped laws and standards; hating ritual constraints on themselves, they cast aside those texts.
translations.idiomatic: But in decline, the lords broke legal norms, resented ritual limits on their power, and discarded the ritual books.

sentences.zh: 遭秦滅學，遂以亂亡。

translations.literal: Then Qin's destruction of learning came, and so all fell into chaos and ruin.
translations.idiomatic: Then Qin suppressed learning, and the tradition collapsed into disorder.

sentences.zh: 漢興，撥亂反正，日不暇給，猶命叔孫通制禮儀，以正君臣之位。

translations.literal: When Han arose and corrected chaos, though pressed day by day with no time to spare, it still ordered Shusun Tong to establish rites and ceremonies, to rectify the positions of ruler and minister.
translations.idiomatic: At the Han founding, even amid urgent state-building, Shusun Tong was tasked with codifying ritual so ruler and minister would each keep their proper place.

sentences.zh: 高祖說而歎曰：「吾乃今日知為天子之貴也！」

translations.literal: Emperor Gaozu rejoiced and sighed: 'Only today do I know the dignity of being Son of Heaven!'
translations.idiomatic: Gaozu exclaimed with delight, 'Only now do I understand the majesty of the Son of Heaven!'

sentences.zh: 以通為奉常，遂定儀法，未盡備而通終。

translations.literal: He appointed Tong as Minister of Ceremonies, and ritual regulations were fixed; before they were fully complete, however, Tong died.
translations.idiomatic: Tong became Minister of Ceremonies and set key protocols, but he died before the system was fully finished.

sentences.zh: 至文帝時，賈誼以為「漢承秦之敗俗，廢禮義，捐廉恥，今其甚者殺父兄，盜者取廟器，而大臣特以簿書不報期會為故，至於風俗流溢，恬而不怪，以為是適然耳。

translations.literal: By Emperor Wen's time, Jia Yi said, 'Han inherited Qin's corrupted customs, discarded ritual and righteousness, and cast away integrity and shame. In the worst cases now, people kill fathers and elder brothers, thieves steal vessels from ancestral temples, yet top ministers treat only failures in clerical reports and missed deadlines as serious. Customs have overflowed to this point: people are numb and unastonished, taking it as merely normal.'
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Wen, Jia Yi argued that Han had inherited Qin's moral collapse: rites, justice, integrity, and shame had eroded so badly that parricide and temple theft appeared, while high officials focused mainly on paperwork and deadlines. Corruption had become so common that people no longer found it shocking.

sentences.zh: 夫移風易俗，使天下回心而鄉道，類非俗吏之所能為也。

translations.literal: To transform customs and turn all under heaven back in heart toward the Way is not something ordinary bureaucrats can do.
translations.idiomatic: Turning the whole empire toward the Way through moral transformation is not work for routine administrators.

sentences.zh: 夫立君臣，等上下，使綱紀有序，六親和睦，此非天之所為，人之所設也。

translations.literal: Establishing ruler and minister, ranking high and low, giving order to social bonds and harmony to the six kinships: this is not done by Heaven, but instituted by human beings.
translations.idiomatic: The ordered relations of ruler and subject, superior and inferior, and harmony among kin are human institutions, not automatic gifts of Heaven.

sentences.zh: 人之所設，不為不立，不修則壞。

translations.literal: What humans institute will not stand if not established, and will decay if not cultivated.
translations.idiomatic: Human institutions must be created and maintained; otherwise they fail.

sentences.zh: 漢興至今二十餘年，宜定制度，興禮樂，然後諸侯軌道，百姓素樸，獄訟衰息」。

translations.literal: From Han's rise until now, more than twenty years have passed; institutions should be fixed and ritual and music revived. Then feudal lords will stay within the proper track, common people will return to plain simplicity, and lawsuits will decline and cease.'
translations.idiomatic: More than twenty years into Han rule, he said, the court should settle institutions and restore rites and music, so the lords remain within bounds, the people return to simplicity, and litigation subsides.

sentences.zh: 乃草具其儀，天子說焉。

translations.literal: He then drafted a rough set of ceremonial forms, and the Son of Heaven approved.
translations.idiomatic: He produced draft ceremonial regulations, and the emperor approved them.

sentences.zh: 而大臣絳、灌之屬害之，故其議遂寢。

translations.literal: But ministers such as Jiang and Guan opposed him, so the proposal was shelved.
translations.idiomatic: Yet senior ministers like Jiang and Guan blocked it, and the plan was dropped.

sentences.zh: 至宣帝時，琅邪王吉為諫大夫，又上疏言：「欲治之主不世出，公卿幸得遭遇其時，未有建萬世之長策，舉明主於三代之隆者也。

translations.literal: By Emperor Xuan's time, Wang Ji of Langye served as Remonstrant Grandee and again submitted a memorial: 'A ruler desirous of good governance does not appear in every age. Though dukes and ministers are fortunate to meet such a time, none has established a long strategy for ten thousand generations or raised a bright ruler to the flourishing standard of the Three Dynasties.'
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Xuan, Wang Ji of Langye, then a remonstrant official, memorialized that truly reforming rulers are rare, yet even in such a moment ministers had failed to devise enduring policy worthy of the Three Dynasties.

sentences.zh: 其務在於簿書斷獄聽訟而已，此非太平之基也。

translations.literal: Their effort lies only in documents, judgments, and lawsuits; this is not the foundation of great peace.
translations.idiomatic: If officials focus only on paperwork and litigation, that cannot be the basis of lasting peace.

sentences.zh: 今俗吏所以牧民者，非有禮義科指可世世通行者也，以意穿鑿，各取一切。

translations.literal: What common officials now use to shepherd the people is not a framework of ritual and righteousness that can be practiced generation after generation; they carve by private ideas, each seizing expedients.
translations.idiomatic: Local administrators lack enduring moral standards; each improvises by personal interpretation and short-term expediency.

sentences.zh: 是以詐偽萌生，刑罰無極，質樸日消，恩愛寖薄。

translations.literal: Therefore fraud and deceit sprout, punishments have no limit, plain simplicity declines day by day, and bonds of affection gradually thin.
translations.idiomatic: As a result, deception multiplies, penalties escalate without end, honesty fades, and human affection steadily weakens.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰『安上治民，莫善於禮』，非空言也。

translations.literal: Confucius said, 'To stabilize those above and govern the people, nothing is better than ritual'; this is not empty speech.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius's claim that ritual best stabilizes authority and orders the people is no empty slogan.

sentences.zh: 願與大臣延及儒生，述舊禮，明王制，驅一世之民，濟之仁壽之域，則俗何以不若成康？

translations.literal: I ask that together with great ministers and extending to ru scholars we recount old rites, clarify kingly institutions, drive the people of this age, and ferry them into a realm of benevolence and long life; then why should customs not equal those under Cheng and Kang?
translations.idiomatic: I ask that ministers and classical scholars together restore ancient rites and clarify royal institutions, guiding the people into a humane and flourishing order; then why could our customs not match the age of Kings Cheng and Kang?

sentences.zh: 壽何以不若高宗？」

translations.literal: And why should longevity not equal Gaozong?'
translations.idiomatic: And why should the dynasty not enjoy longevity like Gaozong's?'

sentences.zh: 上不納其言，吉以病去。

translations.literal: The emperor did not accept his words; Wang Ji left office due to illness.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor rejected his advice, and Wang Ji resigned on grounds of illness.

sentences.zh: 至成帝時，犍為郡於水濱得古磬十六枚，議者以為殒祥。

translations.literal: By Emperor Cheng's time, sixteen ancient stone chimes were found by a riverside in Jianwei Commandery; discussants considered this an auspicious sign.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Cheng, sixteen ancient chime stones were discovered by a river in Jianwei, and many took it as a favorable omen.

sentences.zh: 劉向因是說上：「宜興辟雍，設庠序，陳禮樂，隆雅頌之聲，盛揖攘之容，以風化天下。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang therefore advised the emperor: 'One should establish the Biyong, set up local schools, display ritual and music, exalt the sounds of the Ya and Song hymns, and magnify the forms of bowing and yielding, in order to transform all under heaven.'
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang urged the emperor to build the Biyong and schools, restore ritual music, elevate orthodox hymn traditions, and promote ceremonial civility so as to morally transform the empire.

sentences.zh: 如此而不治者，未之有也。

translations.literal: If one does this and yet fails to govern well, such a case has never existed.
translations.idiomatic: No state has ever remained badly governed after implementing measures like these.

sentences.zh: 或曰，不能具禮。

translations.literal: Some say the full rites cannot be provided.
translations.idiomatic: Some object that the full ritual system cannot be put in place.

sentences.zh: 禮以養人為本，如有過差，是過而養人也。

translations.literal: Ritual takes nurturing people as its root. Even where there is excess or error, it is still an error made while nurturing people.
translations.idiomatic: Ritual is fundamentally about nurturing people; even its mistakes err on the side of care.

sentences.zh: 刑罰之過，或至死傷。

translations.literal: Excess in punishments can sometimes lead to death and injury.
translations.idiomatic: By contrast, excess in criminal law can maim or kill.

sentences.zh: 今之刑，非皋陶之法也，而有司請定法，削則削，筆則筆，救時務也。

translations.literal: Today's punishments are not Gao Yao's laws, yet officials still petition to fix statutes, cutting where they cut and writing where they write, as a way to rescue current affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Today's penal code is no ancient ideal, yet officials constantly revise and enforce it to address immediate needs.

sentences.zh: 至於禮樂，則曰不敢，是敢於殺人不敢於養人也。

translations.literal: But when it comes to ritual and music, they say they dare not. This is daring to kill people but not daring to nurture people.
translations.idiomatic: To enforce punishment while refusing ritual is to be bold in harming and timid in nurturing.

sentences.zh: 為其俎豆筦弦之間小不備，因是絕而不為，是去小不備而就大不備，大不備或莫甚焉。

translations.literal: Because among sacrificial vessels and musical instruments there are minor points not fully prepared, they therefore abandon it entirely and do nothing; this is to leave a small incompleteness for a great incompleteness, and no incompleteness may be worse.
translations.idiomatic: They abandon the whole enterprise because a few ceremonial details are imperfect; that trades minor defects for a far greater deficiency.

sentences.zh: 夫教化之比於刑法，刑法輕，是舍所重而急所輕也。

translations.literal: Compared with penal law, moral education is weightier; penal law is lighter. This is to abandon the weighty and rush toward the light.
translations.idiomatic: Moral transformation is the heavier instrument and punishment the lighter; prioritizing penalties reverses what matters.

sentences.zh: 且教化，所恃以為治也，刑法所以助治也。

translations.literal: Moreover, teaching and transformation are what governance relies on, while penal law is what assists governance.
translations.idiomatic: Instruction and moral transformation are the foundation of rule; criminal law is only an auxiliary support.

sentences.zh: 今廢所恃而獨立其所助，非所以致太平也。

translations.literal: Now to discard what is relied upon and establish only what assists is not the way to bring about great peace.
translations.idiomatic: Discarding the foundation and relying only on penal support cannot produce lasting peace.

sentences.zh: 自京師有誖逆不順之子孫，至於陷大辟受刑戮者不絕，繇不習五常之道也。

translations.literal: From the capital there are rebellious and disobedient sons and grandsons, even to those who fall into capital punishment and execution without end, all because they have not studied the way of the Five Constants.
translations.idiomatic: Even in the capital, unfilial and rebellious descendants keep appearing and ending in execution because they were not formed by the Five Constant virtues.

sentences.zh: 夫承千歲之衰周，繼暴秦之餘敝，民漸漬惡俗，貪饕險詖，不閑義理，不示以大化，而獨敺以刑罰，終已不改。

translations.literal: Inheriting the thousand-year decline of Zhou and succeeding the remaining abuses of violent Qin, the people became steeped in bad customs: greedy, rapacious, perilous, and deceitful, unfamiliar with righteousness and principle. If they are not shown great moral transformation and are driven only by punishments, in the end they still will not change.
translations.idiomatic: After long Zhou decline and Qin's violent legacy, people were soaked in corrosive habits and cut off from moral principle. Without broad ethical cultivation, punishment alone cannot truly reform them.

sentences.zh: 故曰：『

translations.literal: Therefore it is said:
translations.idiomatic: Hence the saying:

sentences.zh: 導之以禮樂，而民和睦。』

translations.literal: 'Guide them with ritual and music, and the people will be harmonious.'
translations.idiomatic: 'Lead the people through rites and music, and they will live in harmony.'

sentences.zh: 初，叔孫通將制定禮儀，見非於齊魯之士，然卒為漢儒宗，業垂後嗣，斯成法也。」

translations.literal: At first, when Shusun Tong was about to establish rites and ceremonies, he was criticized by scholars of Qi and Lu; yet in the end he became the model among Han ru scholars, and his undertaking was handed down to later generations. This is established law.'
translations.idiomatic: When Shusun Tong first drafted ritual institutions, scholars from Qi and Lu attacked him. Yet he became the Han Confucian standard and his work endured through later generations; that is what a lasting institution is.

sentences.zh: 成帝以向言下公卿議，會向病卒，丞相大司空奏請立辟雍。

translations.literal: Emperor Cheng sent Liu Xiang's proposal down for deliberation by ministers. As it happened, Xiang died of illness, and the Chancellor and Grand Minister of Works memorialized requesting the establishment of the Biyong.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Cheng referred Liu Xiang's proposal to the court, but Xiang soon died; afterward the chancellor and the grand minister of works formally requested construction of the Biyong.

sentences.zh: 案行長安城南，營表未作，遭成帝崩，群臣引以定諡。

translations.literal: They surveyed south of Chang'an city, but before construction markers were set, Emperor Cheng died, and ministers diverted to settling his posthumous title.
translations.idiomatic: A site south of Chang'an was surveyed, yet before work began Emperor Cheng died, and officials turned to arranging his posthumous honors.

sentences.zh: 及王莽為宰衡，欲燿眾庶，遂興辟廱，因以篡位，海內畔之。

translations.literal: When Wang Mang became regent, wishing to dazzle the masses, he then built the Biyong and by this usurped the throne; all under heaven rebelled against him.
translations.idiomatic: As regent, Wang Mang built the Biyong to impress the populace, then used the moment to usurp power, provoking empire-wide revolt.

sentences.zh: 世祖受命中興，撥亂反正，改定京師于土中。

translations.literal: When the World-Founder received the mandate and restored the dynasty, he corrected chaos and rectified order, and reset the capital in the central region.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Guangwu restored Han under Heaven's mandate, he reordered the realm and reestablished the capital in the central heartland.

sentences.zh: 即位三十年，四夷賓服，百姓家給，政教清明，乃營立明堂、辟廱。

translations.literal: After thirty years on the throne, with the four border peoples submitting, households well supplied, and governance and education clear, he then built the Mingtang and the Biyong.
translations.idiomatic: After three decades of rule, with frontier peoples pacified and the populace prosperous under lucid governance, he proceeded to build the Mingtang and Biyong.

sentences.zh: 顯宗即位，躬行其禮，宗祀光武皇帝于明堂，養三老五更於辟廱，威儀既盛美矣。

translations.literal: When Emperor Xianzong ascended the throne, he personally practiced these rites: he offered ancestral sacrifice to Emperor Guangwu in the Mingtang and nourished the Three Elders and Five More in the Biyong. The ceremonial forms were then splendid indeed.
translations.idiomatic: On accession, Emperor Ming personally performed these rites: he worshiped Emperor Guangwu in the Mingtang and honored elder worthies in the Biyong, bringing ceremonial magnificence to full display.

sentences.zh: 然德化未流洽者，禮樂未具，群下無所誦說，而庠序尚未設之故也。

translations.literal: Yet moral transformation had not fully spread, because ritual and music were not complete, subordinates had nothing to recite and explain, and schools were still not established.
translations.idiomatic: Even so, moral influence did not fully permeate society, because ritual music remained incomplete, officials lacked a shared canon to expound, and school institutions were still underdeveloped.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：「辟如為山，未成一匱，止，吾止也。」

translations.literal: Confucius said: 'It is like building a mountain: if, before one last basketful, one stops, that stopping is from oneself.'
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said, 'Building a mountain, if you stop with one basket left, the failure is your own stopping.'

sentences.zh: 今叔孫通所撰禮儀，與律令同錄，臧於理官，法家又復不傳。

translations.literal: Now the ritual code compiled by Shusun Tong is filed together with laws and ordinances and stored in judicial offices, and legal officials in turn do not transmit it.
translations.idiomatic: Shusun Tong's ritual code was archived with legal statutes in judicial bureaus, and legal specialists did not pass it down.

sentences.zh: 漢典寢而不著，民臣莫有言者。

translations.literal: Thus Han ceremonial canons gradually vanished from view, and among people and ministers none spoke of them.
translations.idiomatic: As a result, Han ritual institutions faded into obscurity, with scarcely anyone in court or society speaking for them.

sentences.zh: 又通沒之後，河間獻王采禮樂古事，稍稍增輯，至五百餘篇。

translations.literal: After Tong's death, the King Xian of Hejian collected ancient materials on ritual and music and gradually expanded and edited them to more than five hundred chapters.
translations.idiomatic: After Tong died, the Prince Xian of Hejian gathered old ritual and musical records and incrementally compiled them into more than five hundred chapters.

sentences.zh: 今學者不能昭見，但推士禮以及天子，說義又頗謬異，故君臣長幼交接之道娅以不章。

translations.literal: Now scholars cannot clearly apprehend them; they merely infer from shi-level rites up to the Son of Heaven, and their interpretations are often mistaken and divergent. Therefore the way ruler and minister, elder and younger should interact remains unclear.
translations.idiomatic: Today's scholars cannot see the full system clearly; they extrapolate upward from lower-rank rites, and their interpretations diverge badly, leaving core social relations insufficiently defined.

sentences.zh: 樂者，聖人之所樂也，而可以善民心。

translations.literal: Music is what sages delight in, and by it one can make the people's hearts good.
translations.idiomatic: Music was cherished by sages because it can cultivate goodness in the hearts of the people.

sentences.zh: 其感人深，其移風易俗易，故先王著其教焉。

translations.literal: Its capacity to move people is deep, and its transforming of customs is easy; therefore former kings established this teaching.
translations.idiomatic: Music moves people profoundly and reshapes social habits with relative ease; that is why former kings made it central to governance.

sentences.zh: 夫民有血氣心知之性，而無哀樂喜怒之常，應感而動，然後心術形焉。

translations.literal: People possess a nature of blood and vital qi, heart and awareness, but they do not have fixed constancy in sorrow, joy, pleasure, and anger; they move in response to stimuli, and only then are mental tendencies manifested.
translations.idiomatic: Humans possess vital energies and cognition, but emotions are not fixed; they arise in response to circumstances, and through that response the mind's tendencies become visible.

sentences.zh: 是以纖微虍瘁一作「衰」之音作，而民思憂；

translations.literal: Therefore, when subtle, slight, and waning tones are produced, the people incline to worry and sorrow.
translations.idiomatic: Accordingly, thin and fading sounds tend to make people pensive and anxious.

sentences.zh: 闡諧嫚易之音作，而民康樂；

translations.literal: When broad, harmonious, and easy tones are produced, the people become healthy and joyful.
translations.idiomatic: When tones are open, consonant, and relaxed, people become at ease and cheerful.

sentences.zh: 麤厲猛奮之音作，而民剛毅；

translations.literal: When coarse, severe, and forceful tones are produced, the people become firm and resolute.
translations.idiomatic: Harsh, driving sounds foster toughness and determination.

sentences.zh: 廉直正誠之音作，而民肅敬；

translations.literal: When incorrupt, straight, correct, and sincere tones are produced, the people become solemn and reverent.
translations.idiomatic: Upright and sincere tones cultivate gravity and respect.

sentences.zh: 寬裕和順之音作，而民慈愛；

translations.literal: When broad, ample, and gentle tones are produced, the people become compassionate and loving.
translations.idiomatic: Generous and mild tones encourage kindness and affection.

sentences.zh: 流辟邪散之音作，而民淫亂。

translations.literal: When dissipated, deviant, and scattered tones are produced, the people become licentious and disorderly.
translations.idiomatic: Loose and perverse sounds lead people toward indulgence and disorder.

sentences.zh: 先王恥其亂也，故制雅頌之聲，本之情性，稽之度數，制之禮儀，合生氣之和，導五常之行，使之陽而不散，陰而不集，剛氣不怒，柔氣不懾，四暢交於中，而發作於外，皆安其位而不相奪也，足以感動人之善心而，不使邪氣得接焉，是先王立樂之方也。

translations.literal: The former kings were ashamed of such disorder; therefore they fashioned the sounds of Ya and Song, grounding them in human feelings and nature, testing them by measures and numbers, regulating them by ritual forms, joining them with the harmony of vital energies, and guiding the practice of the Five Constants, so that yang would be active without dissipation, yin gathered without stagnation, hard qi not enraged, soft qi not cowed, the four influences flowing freely within and expressing outwardly, each abiding in its place without encroaching on another. Thus they could stir people's good hearts and keep depraved influences from entering. This was the method by which former kings established music.
translations.idiomatic: Ashamed of moral chaos, former kings created orthodox music, calibrated by measure and ritual, aligned with human feeling and cosmic harmony, and directed toward the Five Constant virtues. Properly balanced, it animated without excess, softened without weakness, kept forces in due proportion, awakened goodness, and blocked corrupt influences; this was their method for instituting music.

sentences.zh: 王者未作樂之時，因先王之樂以教化百姓，說樂其俗，然後改作，以章功德。

translations.literal: Before a king creates new music, he uses earlier kings' music to instruct and transform the people, delighting and harmonizing customs; only then does he revise and create anew, to manifest his achievements and virtue.
translations.idiomatic: A ruler first governs with inherited music to educate the populace and refine customs, then composes new works to mark his own merit and virtue.

sentences.zh: 《易》曰：「先王以作樂崇德，殷薦之上帝，以配祖考。」

translations.literal: The Changes says: 'The former kings used music to exalt virtue, solemnly presenting it to the High Thearch and pairing it with their ancestral forebears.'
translations.idiomatic: As the Changes says, former kings made music to honor virtue, offering it reverently to the Supreme Deity and in accompaniment to ancestral worship.

sentences.zh: 昔黃帝作咸池，顓頊作六莖，帝嚳作五英，堯作大章，舜作招，禹作夏，湯作濩，武王作武，周公作勺。

translations.literal: In antiquity, the Yellow Emperor made Xianchi; Zhuanxu made Liujing; Emperor Ku made Wuying; Yao made Dazhang; Shun made Zhao; Yu made Xia; Tang made Huo; King Wu made Wu; and the Duke of Zhou made Shao.
translations.idiomatic: The tradition names these foundational compositions: Xianchi of the Yellow Emperor, Liujing of Zhuanxu, Wuying of Emperor Ku, Dazhang of Yao, Zhao of Shun, Xia of Yu, Huo of Tang, Wu of King Wu, and Shao of the Duke of Zhou.

sentences.zh: 勺，言能勺先祖之道也。

translations.literal: Shao means being able to draw out and continue the way of the ancestors.
translations.idiomatic: 'Shao' signifies carrying forward the ancestral way.

sentences.zh: 武，言以功定天下也。

translations.literal: Wu means pacifying all under heaven through martial achievement.
translations.idiomatic: 'Wu' signifies bringing the realm to order through military merit.

sentences.zh: 濩，言救民也。

translations.literal: Huo means rescuing the people.
translations.idiomatic: 'Huo' signifies saving the people.

sentences.zh: 夏，大承二帝也。

translations.literal: Xia means greatly inheriting the two preceding sovereigns.
translations.idiomatic: 'Xia' signifies grandly continuing the legacy of two earlier emperors.

sentences.zh: 招，繼堯也。

translations.literal: Zhao means succeeding Yao.
translations.idiomatic: 'Zhao' signifies carrying on from Yao.

sentences.zh: 大章，章之也。

translations.literal: Dazhang means making it manifest.
translations.idiomatic: 'Dazhang' signifies grand manifestation.

sentences.zh: 五英，英華茂也。

translations.literal: Wuying means flourishing brilliance.
translations.idiomatic: 'Wuying' signifies luxuriant brilliance.

sentences.zh: 六莖，及根莖也。

translations.literal: Liujing means extending even to roots and stalks.
translations.idiomatic: 'Liujing' signifies reaching all the way to the roots.

sentences.zh: 咸池，備矣。

translations.literal: Xianchi means complete fullness.
translations.idiomatic: 'Xianchi' signifies full completeness.

sentences.zh: 自夏以往，其流不可聞已，殷頌猶有存者。

translations.literal: From Xia onward, their later developments can no longer be heard; only some Yin hymns still survive.
translations.idiomatic: After Xia, the later streams are no longer recoverable in sound, with only remnants of Yin hymns still preserved.

sentences.zh: 周詩既備，而其器用張陳，周官具焉。

translations.literal: The Zhou odes were complete, and their instruments and ceremonial display are fully set forth in the Offices of Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: By Zhou times the poetic corpus was complete, and the arrangement of instruments and ritual display was systematically codified in the Zhou offices.

sentences.zh: 典者自卿大夫師瞽以下，皆選有道德之人，朝夕習業，以教國子。

translations.literal: As for officials, from high music officers down to masters and blind musicians, all selected men of virtue; morning and evening they trained in their duties to instruct the state sons.
translations.idiomatic: Music officials at every level were chosen for moral character and trained constantly so they could educate the state's young elites.

sentences.zh: 國子者，卿大夫之子弟也。

translations.literal: The state sons were the sons and younger kinsmen of high ministers and grandees.
translations.idiomatic: These 'state sons' were the sons and younger relatives of major officials.

sentences.zh: 皆學歌九德，誦六詩，習六舞、五聲、八音之和。

translations.literal: All of them studied singing the Nine Virtues, reciting the Six Poems, and practicing the Six Dances, along with the harmonies of the Five Tones and Eight Sounds.
translations.idiomatic: They were all trained to sing the Nine Virtues, recite the Six Poems, and perform the Six Dances in proper accord with the Five Tones and Eight Instrumental Sounds.

sentences.zh: 故帝舜命夔曰：「女典樂，教冑子，直而溫，寬而栗，剛而無虐，簡而無敖。

translations.literal: Therefore Emperor Shun commanded Kui, saying: 'You are in charge of music. Teach the noble youths to be upright yet warm, broad yet reverent, firm yet not harsh, simple yet not arrogant.'
translations.idiomatic: Thus Emperor Shun charged Kui with musical education, instructing him to form noble youths who were upright but gentle, broad-minded yet disciplined, strong without cruelty, and plain without arrogance.

sentences.zh: 詩言志，歌詠言，聲依詠，律和聲，八音克諧。」

translations.literal: Poetry speaks intent, song chants words, tones follow the chant, pitch-pipes harmonize the tones, and the eight sounds can be brought into harmony."
translations.idiomatic: "Poems express intention; songs extend words; tones follow the chant; pitch standards tune the tones; and all eight classes of instruments can be harmonized."

sentences.zh: 此之謂也。

translations.literal: This is what it means.
translations.idiomatic: This is exactly what that saying refers to.

sentences.zh: 又以外賞諸侯德盛而教尊者。

translations.literal: Moreover, by this one externally rewards the feudal lords whose virtue is abundant and whose teaching is honored.
translations.idiomatic: It also serves to honor and reward those feudal lords who are rich in virtue and esteemed for their moral instruction.

sentences.zh: 其威儀足以充目，音聲足以動耳，詩語足以感心，故聞其音而德和，省其詩而志正，論其數而法立。

translations.literal: Its majestic forms are enough to fill the eyes, its sounds are enough to move the ears, and its poetic words are enough to stir the heart. Therefore, hearing its sounds harmonizes virtue, examining its poems corrects intention, and discussing its measures establishes standards.
translations.idiomatic: Its ceremony satisfies the eye, its music moves the ear, and its verse touches the heart. So its sounds harmonize character, its poems straighten intent, and its patterned measures ground social norms.

sentences.zh: 是以薦之郊廟則鬼神饗，作之朝廷則群臣和，立之學官則萬民協。

translations.literal: Thus, when it is offered in suburban and ancestral temples, spirits partake; when it is performed in court, the ministers are harmonious; when it is established in state schools, the myriad people are in accord.
translations.idiomatic: So when these rites and music are offered at state and ancestral sacrifices, spirits are received; when practiced at court, officials are unified; when taught in schools, the people come into concord.

sentences.zh: 聽者無不虛己竦神，說而承流，是以海內遍知上德，被服其風，光煇日新，化上遷善，而不知所以然，至於萬物不夭，天地順而嘉應降。

translations.literal: Those who listen all empty themselves and rouse their spirits, delighting in it and receiving its current. Therefore, throughout the realm all come to know higher virtue and are transformed by its influence; radiance is renewed day by day, people move upward toward goodness without knowing why; and even to the point that things do not wither prematurely, Heaven and Earth are in accord, and auspicious responses descend.
translations.idiomatic: All who hear it set aside self-interest and are inwardly stirred, gladly following its moral current. In this way the whole realm learns higher virtue and is shaped by it: culture brightens day by day, people rise toward goodness almost without noticing, and harmony becomes so deep that even nature seems to respond with favorable signs.

sentences.zh: 故《詩》曰：「鐘鼓鍠鍠，磬管鏘鏘，降福穰穰。」

translations.literal: Therefore the Odes say: 'Bells and drums resound huang-huang, chimes and pipes ring qiang-qiang, and blessings descend in abundance.'
translations.idiomatic: Hence the Odes say: 'Bells and drums ring out, chimes and flutes answer, and blessings pour down in plenty.'

sentences.zh: 《書》云：「擊石拊石，百獸率舞。」

translations.literal: The Documents say: 'Strike the stone chimes and tap the stones, and the hundred beasts all dance in unison.'
translations.idiomatic: As the Documents put it: 'When the stone instruments are sounded, all creatures move together in dance.'

sentences.zh: 鳥獸且猶感應，而況於人乎？

translations.literal: If even birds and beasts can be moved and respond, how much more so humans?
translations.idiomatic: If even animals are moved by it, how much more deeply should people be moved?

sentences.zh: 況於鬼神乎？

translations.literal: How much more so spirits?
translations.idiomatic: And how much more would this affect the spirits?

sentences.zh: 故樂者，聖人之所以感天地，通神明，安萬民，成性類者也。

translations.literal: Therefore music is that by which sages move Heaven and Earth, communicate with the numinous, settle the myriad people, and complete the kinds of human nature.
translations.idiomatic: Thus music is the means by which sages resonate with heaven and earth, commune with the spiritual order, bring peace to the people, and fully cultivate human nature.

sentences.zh: 然自雅頌之興，而所承衰亂之音猶在，是謂淫過凶嫚之聲，為設禁焉。

translations.literal: Yet even after the rise of the Ya and Song hymns, sounds inherited from ages of decline and disorder still remained. These are called licentious, excessive, violent, and arrogant sounds, and prohibitions were established against them.
translations.idiomatic: But even with the rise of orthodox hymn traditions, corrupted music from eras of decline still persisted. Such sounds, marked by excess and brutality, were therefore explicitly forbidden.

sentences.zh: 世衰民散，小人乘君子，心耳淺薄，則邪勝正。

translations.literal: When the age declines and people disperse, petty men overrun gentlemen; when hearts and ears are shallow and thin, the deviant overcomes the upright.
translations.idiomatic: In times of decay, social bonds fray and petty men overpower principled ones; when people grow shallow in sensibility, the perverse defeats the proper.

sentences.zh: 故書序「殷紂斷棄先祖之樂，乃作淫聲，用變亂正聲，以說婦人。」

translations.literal: Therefore the Book Preface says: 'King Zhou of Yin cut off and abandoned the music of his forefathers, then made licentious sounds, using them to alter and disorder the correct tones, to please women.'
translations.idiomatic: Hence the preface to the Documents says that Yin's King Zhou cast aside ancestral music and created depraved sounds, distorting orthodox tones for sensual pleasure.

sentences.zh: 樂官師瞽抱其器而奔散，或適諸侯，或入河海。

translations.literal: Music officers and blind masters fled in scattered groups carrying their instruments; some went to feudal lords, others disappeared into the rivers and seas.
translations.idiomatic: Court musicians and blind music masters fled with their instruments in chaos, some taking service with regional lords, others vanishing into exile.

sentences.zh: 夫樂本情性，浹肌膚而臧骨髓，雖經乎千載，其遺風餘烈尚猶不絕。

translations.literal: Music is rooted in human feeling and nature; it permeates skin and flesh and is stored in bone and marrow. Though a thousand years pass, its remaining influence and residual force still do not cease.
translations.idiomatic: Music arises from human nature itself. It sinks into the body and lodges deep in the marrow, so even after a thousand years its lingering power still does not disappear.

sentences.zh: 至春秋時，陳公子完奔齊。

translations.literal: By the Spring and Autumn period, Prince Wan of Chen fled to Qi.
translations.idiomatic: In the Spring and Autumn era, Prince Wan of Chen went into exile in Qi.

sentences.zh: 陳，舜之後，招樂存焉。

translations.literal: Chen was descended from Shun, and the Zhao music was preserved there.
translations.idiomatic: Because Chen traced its lineage to Shun, the ancient Zhao music tradition was still preserved there.

sentences.zh: 故孔子適齊聞招，三月不知肉味，曰「不圖為樂之至於斯！」

translations.literal: Therefore when Confucius went to Qi and heard the Zhao music, for three months he did not know the taste of meat, saying, 'I never imagined music could reach this point!'
translations.idiomatic: So when Confucius visited Qi and heard the Zhao music, he was so absorbed that for three months he forgot the taste of meat, exclaiming, 'I never imagined music could attain such heights!'

sentences.zh: 美之甚也。

translations.literal: He praised it to the utmost.
translations.idiomatic: This shows how deeply he admired it.

sentences.zh: 周道始缺，怨刺之詩起。

translations.literal: When the Way of Zhou first began to decline, poems of complaint and remonstrance arose.
translations.idiomatic: As the Zhou order began to fray, poetry of grievance and criticism emerged.

sentences.zh: 王澤既竭，而詩不能作。

translations.literal: When the royal beneficence was exhausted, poetry could no longer be produced.
translations.idiomatic: Once royal virtue was spent, the poetic tradition itself could no longer be renewed.

sentences.zh: 王官失業，雅頌相錯，孔子論而定之，故曰：「吾自衛反魯，然後樂正，雅頌各得其所。」

translations.literal: Royal officials lost their duties, and the Ya and Song became intermingled. Confucius discussed and fixed them; therefore he said, 'After I returned to Lu from Wei, only then was music rectified, and Ya and Song each obtained its proper place.'
translations.idiomatic: When court musicians lost their proper functions, the Ya and Song traditions became confused. Confucius reclassified and stabilized them, hence his remark that only after returning from Wei to Lu did music become correct and each canon return to its proper place.

sentences.zh: 是時，周室大壞，諸侯恣行，設兩觀，乘大路。

translations.literal: At that time the Zhou house was in great collapse; the feudal lords acted wantonly, setting up paired gate-towers and riding on the great road.
translations.idiomatic: By then the Zhou royal order had badly broken down: regional lords behaved without restraint, usurping royal architectural and ceremonial privileges.

sentences.zh: 陪臣管仲、季氏之屬，三歸雍徹，八佾舞廷。

translations.literal: Subordinate ministers such as Guan Zhong and men of the Ji lineage used threefold return rites with Yong music at completion and had an eight-row dance performed in the courtyard.
translations.idiomatic: Even sub-ministers like Guan Zhong and the Ji clan appropriated high ritual forms, including grand completion rites and the eight-rank court dance reserved for higher authority.

sentences.zh: 制度遂壞，陵夷而不反，桑間、濮上，鄭、衛、宋、趙之聲並出，內則致疾損壽，外則亂政傷民。

translations.literal: Institutions then collapsed, declined step by step without returning; the sounds of Sangjian and Pushang, and of Zheng, Wei, Song, and Zhao all emerged together. Inwardly they caused illness and shortened life; outwardly they disordered government and harmed the people.
translations.idiomatic: Institutional order decayed and never recovered. Erotic and disorderly musical styles spread everywhere, and their effects were corrosive both personally and politically: they damaged health and longevity, and they destabilized governance while harming the people.

sentences.zh: 巧偽因而飾之，以營亂富貴之耳目。

translations.literal: Clever falsifiers then embellished these, using them to seduce and disorder the ears and eyes of the wealthy and noble.
translations.idiomatic: Cunning opportunists polished these corrupt forms and used them to captivate and confuse elite taste.

sentences.zh: 庶人以求利，列國以相間。

translations.literal: Commoners used them to seek profit, and the various states used them to spy on one another.
translations.idiomatic: Common people exploited them for gain, while rival states used them as tools of political penetration and intrigue.

sentences.zh: 故秦穆遺戎而由余去，齊人餽魯而孔子行。

translations.literal: Therefore Duke Mu of Qin neglected the Rong and You Yu departed; and when the people of Qi sent gifts to Lu, Confucius left.
translations.idiomatic: Thus Qin's Duke Mu, diverted by such influences, lost You Yu; likewise, when Qi's gifts corrupted Lu, Confucius chose to depart.

sentences.zh: 至於六國，魏文侯最為好古，而謂子夏曰：「寡人聽古樂則欲寐，及聞鄭、衛，余不知倦焉。」

translations.literal: As for the Six States period, Marquis Wen of Wei was the most fond of antiquity, yet he said to Zixia: 'When I listen to ancient music I become sleepy; when I hear Zheng and Wei music, I do not know fatigue.'
translations.idiomatic: By the Warring States age, even Marquis Wen of Wei, though known for honoring antiquity, told Zixia: 'Ancient music makes me drowsy, but the music of Zheng and Wei never lets me tire.'

sentences.zh: 子夏辭而辨之，終不見納，自此禮樂喪矣。

translations.literal: Zixia spoke and argued it out, but in the end he was not accepted. From this point, ritual and music were lost.
translations.idiomatic: Zixia defended orthodox music in detail, but his argument was rejected; from then on, the ritual-musical tradition declined.

sentences.zh: 漢興，樂家有制氏，以雅樂聲律世世在大樂官，但能紀其鏗鎗鼓舞，而不能言其義。

translations.literal: When Han arose, among musical families there was the Zhi clan, who for generations remained in the Grand Music Office with the pitches and regulations of elegant music; they could only record its ringing sounds and drum-dances, but could not explain its meaning.
translations.idiomatic: After the Han founding, hereditary court musicians preserved technical pitch systems within the Grand Music Office, but they could transmit performance forms more than interpretive meaning.

sentences.zh: 高祖時，叔孫通因秦樂人制宗廟樂。

translations.literal: In Gaozu's reign, Shusun Tong relied on Qin musicians to establish music for the ancestral temples.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Gaozu, Shusun Tong used Qin court musicians as a basis for restoring temple music.

sentences.zh: 大祝迎神于廟門，奏嘉至，猶古降神之樂也。

translations.literal: The Grand Invocator welcomed the spirits at the temple gate and played Jiazhi, like the ancient music for descending spirits.
translations.idiomatic: At the temple gate, the chief ritual officiant greeted the spirits to the piece Jiazhi, corresponding to the old 'inviting down the spirits' music.

sentences.zh: 皇帝入廟門，奏永至，以為行步之節，猶古采薺、肆夏也。

translations.literal: When the emperor entered the temple gate, Yongzhi was played, serving as the measure for his steps, like the ancient Caiji and Sixia pieces.
translations.idiomatic: As the emperor entered, Yongzhi marked the cadence of his movement, functioning like the ancient processional pieces Caiji and Sixia.

sentences.zh: 乾豆上，奏登歌，獨上歌，不以筦弦亂人聲，欲在位者遍聞之，猶古清廟之歌也。

translations.literal: When the dried offerings were presented, ascending hymns were played; only a lead vocal was sung, not using pipes and strings to disorder the human voice, wishing those in attendance all to hear it, like the ancient song of the Qing Temple.
translations.idiomatic: At the offering of the dry sacrificial dishes, the ascension hymn was sung as unaccompanied lead chant, without winds or strings obscuring the human voice, so everyone present could clearly hear it, in the manner of the ancient Qingmiao hymn.

sentences.zh: 登歌再終，下奏休成之樂，美神明既饗也。

translations.literal: After the ascending hymn ended twice, below they played Xiucheng music, praising that the spirits had already partaken.
translations.idiomatic: After two cycles of the ascension chant, the ensemble below performed Xiucheng to celebrate that the spirits had accepted the offering.

sentences.zh: 皇帝就酒東廂，坐定，奏永安之樂，美禮已成也。

translations.literal: When the emperor moved to the wine station in the eastern side chamber and took his seat, Yong'an music was played, praising that the rite had been completed.
translations.idiomatic: Once the emperor took his seat at the eastern wine position, Yong'an was performed to mark the successful completion of the ceremony.

sentences.zh: 又有房中祠樂，高祖唐山夫人所作也。

translations.literal: There was also inner-chamber sacrificial music, composed by Lady Tangshan of Emperor Gaozu.
translations.idiomatic: There was also an inner-chamber liturgical repertoire, attributed to Lady Tangshan in Gaozu's time.

sentences.zh: 周有房中樂，至秦名曰壽人。

translations.literal: Zhou had inner-chamber music; by Qin it was named Shouren.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhou tradition already had inner-chamber music, which in Qin times was called Shouren.

sentences.zh: 凡樂，樂其所生，禮不忘本。

translations.literal: In all music, one values what gave rise to it; in ritual, one does not forget the root.
translations.idiomatic: The principle is that music should honor its origin, and ritual should never lose sight of first principles.

sentences.zh: 高祖樂楚聲，故房中樂楚聲也。

translations.literal: Gaozu delighted in Chu sounds, therefore the inner-chamber music used Chu sounds.
translations.idiomatic: Because Gaozu favored the musical style of Chu, the inner-chamber repertoire adopted Chu tonal character.

sentences.zh: 孝惠二年，使樂府令夏侯寬備其簫管，更名曰安世樂。

translations.literal: In the second year of Emperor Hui, he ordered Xiahou Kuan, Director of the Music Bureau, to complete its flutes and pipes, and renamed it Anshi Music.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Hui's second year, Music Bureau Director Xiahou Kuan regularized its wind instrumentation and retitled the suite 'Music for Pacifying the Age.'

sentences.zh: 高祖廟奏武德、文始、五行之舞；

translations.literal: At Gaozu's temple they performed the dances Wude, Wenshi, and Wuxing;
translations.idiomatic: At Gaozu's ancestral temple, the court performed the Wude, Wenshi, and Five-Phases dances.

sentences.zh: 孝文廟奏昭德、文始、四時、五行之舞；

translations.literal: At Emperor Wen's temple they performed the dances Zhaode, Wenshi, Sishi, and Wuxing;
translations.idiomatic: At Emperor Wen's temple they performed Zhaode, Wenshi, Four-Seasons, and Five-Phases dances.

sentences.zh: 孝武廟奏盛德、文始、四時、五行之舞。

translations.literal: At Emperor Wu's temple they performed the Shengde, Wenshi, Four Seasons, and Five Phases dances.
translations.idiomatic: At Emperor Wu's ancestral temple, the court performed Shengde, Wenshi, Four-Seasons, and Five-Phases dances.

sentences.zh: 武德舞者，高祖四年作，以象天下樂己行武以除亂也。

translations.literal: The Wude dance was created in Gaozu's fourth year, symbolizing the realm's joy that he used military force to end disorder.
translations.idiomatic: The Wude dance was instituted in Gaozu's fourth year to represent the empire's gratitude for pacification through force.

sentences.zh: 文始舞者，曰本舜招舞也，高祖六年更名曰文始，以示不相襲也。

translations.literal: The Wenshi dance was originally said to be Shun's Zhao dance; in Gaozu's sixth year it was renamed Wenshi to show it was not a direct imitation.
translations.idiomatic: Wenshi was traced to Shun's Zhao dance, but it was renamed in Gaozu's sixth year to mark a distinct Han adaptation.

sentences.zh: 五行舞者，本周舞也，秦始皇二十六年更名曰五行也。

translations.literal: The Five Phases dance was originally a Zhou dance; in Qin Shihuang's twenty-sixth year it was renamed Five Phases.
translations.idiomatic: The Five-Phases dance came from Zhou precedent and was retitled in the twenty-sixth year of the First Emperor of Qin.

sentences.zh: 四時舞者，孝文所作，以明示天下之安和也。

translations.literal: The Four Seasons dance was created by Emperor Wen to clearly display peace and harmony to the realm.
translations.idiomatic: The Four-Seasons dance was composed under Emperor Wen to proclaim orderly peace throughout the empire.

sentences.zh: 蓋樂己所自作，明有制也；

translations.literal: To value one's own composed music shows there are institutions;
translations.idiomatic: Honoring music one creates oneself demonstrates deliberate institutional design;

sentences.zh: 樂先王之樂，明有法也。

translations.literal: To value the former kings' music shows there are models.
translations.idiomatic: Honoring the music of former kings shows fidelity to normative models.

sentences.zh: 孝景采武德舞以為昭德，以尊大宗廟。

translations.literal: Emperor Jing adopted the Wude dance and made it Zhaode, to honor the great ancestral temple.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Jing adapted Wude into Zhaode to elevate the primary ancestral shrine.

sentences.zh: 至孝宣，采昭德舞為盛德，以尊世宗廟。

translations.literal: By Emperor Xuan, Zhaode was adopted as Shengde, to honor the Shizong temple.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Xuan, Zhaode was transformed into Shengde to honor Shizong's temple cult.

sentences.zh: 諸帝廟皆常奏文始、四時、五行舞云。

translations.literal: At all imperial temples, Wenshi, Four Seasons, and Five Phases dances were regularly performed.
translations.idiomatic: Across the imperial ancestral temples, Wenshi plus the Four-Seasons and Five-Phases dances became standard repertory.

sentences.zh: 高祖六年又作昭容樂、禮容樂。

translations.literal: In Gaozu's sixth year, Zhaorong music and Lirong music were also created.
translations.idiomatic: Gaozu's sixth year also saw the creation of the Zhaorong and Lirong ceremonial pieces.

sentences.zh: 昭容者，猶古之昭夏也，主出武德舞。

translations.literal: Zhaorong was like the ancient Zhaoxia and mainly accompanied the Wude dance.
translations.idiomatic: Zhaorong corresponded to the old Zhaoxia type and chiefly introduced the Wude dance.

sentences.zh: 禮容者，主出文始、五行舞。

translations.literal: Lirong mainly accompanied the Wenshi and Five Phases dances.
translations.idiomatic: Lirong functioned primarily with the Wenshi and Five-Phases dance sets.

sentences.zh: 舞人無樂者，將至至尊之前不敢以樂也；

translations.literal: When dancers were without music, it meant that before the supreme one they dared not use music;
translations.idiomatic: Dancing without accompaniment signaled reverent restraint before the supreme ruler;

sentences.zh: 出用樂者，言舞不失節，能以樂終也。

translations.literal: Using music on exit meant that the dance had not lost proper measure and could conclude through music.
translations.idiomatic: Music at the exit marked that the dance had maintained ritual measure and reached a proper musical close.

sentences.zh: 大氐皆因秦舊事焉。

translations.literal: For the most part, all this followed old Qin practice.
translations.idiomatic: In broad outline, these arrangements inherited Qin precedents.

sentences.zh: 初，高祖既定天下，過沛，與故人父老相樂，醉酒歡哀，作「風起」之詩，令沛中僮兒百二十人習而歌之。

translations.literal: At first, after Gaozu pacified the realm, he passed through Pei and celebrated with old friends and elders; drunk and deeply moved, he composed the poem "The Wind Rises" and ordered 120 boys of Pei to learn and sing it.
translations.idiomatic: After unifying the empire, Gaozu visited Pei, celebrated with his old community, and in drunken emotion wrote "The Wind Rises," assigning 120 local youths to perform it.

sentences.zh: 至孝惠時，以沛宮為原廟，皆令歌兒習吹以相和，常以百二十人為員。

translations.literal: By Emperor Hui's time, Pei Palace was made an original temple, and singing boys were all trained in wind accompaniment; the number was regularly set at 120.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Hui, Pei Palace became an ancestral shrine, and 120 choir youths were routinely trained to sing with instrumental accompaniment.

sentences.zh: 文、景之間，禮官肄業而已。

translations.literal: Between Emperor Wen and Emperor Jing, ritual officials only rehearsed these matters.
translations.idiomatic: Between the Wen and Jing reigns, ritual offices largely maintained practice through rehearsal alone.

sentences.zh: 至武帝定郊祀之禮，祠太一於甘泉，就乾位也；

translations.literal: When Emperor Wu fixed the suburban-sacrifice rites, he sacrificed to Taiyi at Ganquan, taking the Qian position;
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Wu formalized suburban sacrifice, he worshiped Taiyi at Ganquan in the celestial Qian orientation;

sentences.zh: 祭后土於汾陰，澤中方丘也。

translations.literal: and sacrificed to Houtu at Fenyin, at the square mound in the marsh.
translations.idiomatic: and he offered to Houtu at Fenyin, at the square altar within the marshland.

sentences.zh: 乃立樂府，采詩夜誦，有趙、代、秦、楚之謳。

translations.literal: Then he established the Music Bureau, collecting poems and night recitations, including songs of Zhao, Dai, Qin, and Chu.
translations.idiomatic: He then founded the Music Bureau, gathering sung texts and nocturnal chants, including regional songs from Zhao, Dai, Qin, and Chu.

sentences.zh: 以李延年為協律都尉，多舉司馬相如等數十人造為詩賦，略論律呂，以合八音之調，作十九章之歌。

translations.literal: Li Yannian was made Commandant Harmonizing Pitch, and dozens such as Sima Xiangru were selected to compose poems and fu; they discussed pitch standards in outline, matched the tuning of the eight sounds, and made a nineteen-chapter song cycle.
translations.idiomatic: With Li Yannian appointed to oversee pitch, court literati including Sima Xiangru composed new lyrics, aligning pitch theory and instrumental tuning into a nineteen-part liturgical cycle.

sentences.zh: 以正月上辛用事甘泉圜丘，使童男女七十人俱歌，昏祠至明。

translations.literal: On the first xin day of the first month, rites were conducted at Ganquan Round Mound, with seventy boys and girls singing together from dusk sacrifice to dawn.
translations.idiomatic: At the upper-xin day rites of the first month at Ganquan's round altar, seventy youths sang continuously from night offering through daybreak.

sentences.zh: 夜常有神光如流星止集于祠壇，天子自竹宮而望拜，百官侍祠者數百人皆肅然動心焉。

translations.literal: At night, divine light often like meteors gathered on the altar; the Son of Heaven watched and bowed from the bamboo palace, and hundreds of attending officials were all solemnly moved.
translations.idiomatic: Nights were said to show meteor-like sacred light settling on the altar; from the bamboo precinct the emperor bowed in reverence, and hundreds of attendants were deeply stirred.

sentences.zh: 安世房中歌十七章，其詩曰：

translations.literal: The "Anshi Inner-Chamber Songs" had seventeen sections; their verses say:
translations.idiomatic: The "Anshi Chamber Hymns" consisted of seventeen stanzas, beginning as follows:

sentences.zh: 大孝備矣，休德昭清。

translations.literal: Great filial piety is complete; auspicious virtue shines pure and clear.
translations.idiomatic: Filial piety is fulfilled; beneficent virtue stands luminous and pure.

sentences.zh: 高張四縣，樂充宮庭。

translations.literal: The four suspended instruments are raised high; music fills palace courts.
translations.idiomatic: The four suspended ensembles are lifted high, and music saturates the palace court.

sentences.zh: 芬樹羽林，雲景杳冥，金支秀華，庶旄翠旌。

translations.literal: Fragrant plumes line feathered guards; clouded radiance is deep and dim; golden branches bloom in splendor, and many yak-tail banners are emerald bright.
translations.idiomatic: Fragrant standards and feathered arrays glow beneath shadowed cloud-light; golden ornament flourishes amid green-plumed banners.

sentences.zh: 七始華始，肅倡和聲。

translations.literal: Seven beginnings, splendid beginning; solemnly begin harmonious sound.
translations.idiomatic: At the sevenfold opening, a radiant beginning: solemn voices initiate concordant tones.

sentences.zh: 神來宴娭，庶幾是聽。

translations.literal: The spirits come to feast and rejoice; perhaps they are listening.
translations.idiomatic: The spirits arrive to banquet and delight; may they indeed be listening.

sentences.zh: 粥粥音送，細齊人情。

translations.literal: Softly, softly the sounds are sent forth, subtly ordering human feeling.
translations.idiomatic: Gently the tones proceed, finely attuning the emotions of humankind.

sentences.zh: 忽乘青玄，熙事備成。

translations.literal: Suddenly riding dark azure, the grand rite is fully completed.
translations.idiomatic: Suddenly borne on deep-blue force, the splendid ceremony reaches completion.

sentences.zh: 清思眑眑，經緯冥冥。

translations.literal: Pure thought is deep and dim; warp and weft are obscurely ordered.
translations.idiomatic: Thought turns pure and profound as hidden patterns are silently woven.

sentences.zh: 我定曆數，人告其心。

translations.literal: We set the calendar numbers; the people declare their hearts.
translations.idiomatic: We establish calendrical order, and the people voice what lies in their hearts.

sentences.zh: 敕身齊戒，施教申申。

translations.literal: Admonish the body and complete purification; extend instruction again and again.
translations.idiomatic: Discipline oneself, complete fasting and purification, and press moral teaching onward.

sentences.zh: 乃立祖廟，敬明尊親。

translations.literal: Then establish the ancestral temple, reverently illuminating honored kin.
translations.idiomatic: Then found the ancestral shrine and reverently honor the lineage.

sentences.zh: 大矣孝熙，四極爰轃。

translations.literal: Great indeed is filial brilliance; it reaches the four extremities.
translations.idiomatic: Vast is filial radiance, extending to the four corners of the world.

sentences.zh: 王侯秉德，其鄰翼翼，顯明昭式。

translations.literal: Kings and lords hold virtue; their neighboring states stand orderly; bright models are made clear.
translations.idiomatic: Princes uphold virtue, neighboring polities align in order, and exemplary norms shine forth.

sentences.zh: 清明鬯矣，皇帝孝德。

translations.literal: Clarity and purity are abundant; the emperor's filial virtue is manifest.
translations.idiomatic: Purity and lucidity abound, revealing the emperor's filial virtue.

sentences.zh: 竟全大功，撫安四極。

translations.literal: At last the great achievement is complete, pacifying and securing the four quarters.
translations.idiomatic: The great work is brought to completion, calming and securing all four directions.

sentences.zh: 海內有姦，紛亂東北。

translations.literal: Within the seas there is treachery, with turmoil in the northeast.
translations.idiomatic: Treachery appeared within the realm, and unrest flared in the northeast.

sentences.zh: 詔撫成師，武臣承德。

translations.literal: An edict soothed and completed the armies; martial ministers upheld virtue.
translations.idiomatic: By imperial command the armies were pacified and martial officials carried out virtuous order.

sentences.zh: 行樂交逆，簫、勺群慝。

translations.literal: Marching music met rebellion; Xiao and Shao subdued the many evils.
translations.idiomatic: Ceremonial-military music confronted insurgency, subduing manifold disorder.

sentences.zh: 肅為濟哉，蓋定燕國。

translations.literal: Solemnly this brought relief, thereby settling Yan territory.
translations.idiomatic: Through solemn intervention the crisis was eased and Yan was stabilized.

sentences.zh: 大海蕩蕩水所歸，高賢愉愉民所懷。

translations.literal: The great sea is vast where waters return; the great worthy is serene where the people place their hopes.
translations.idiomatic: As all waters return to the sea, so the people gather their trust around great worthies.

sentences.zh: 大山崔，百卉殖。

translations.literal: The great mountain stands lofty; a hundred plants flourish.
translations.idiomatic: The high mountain towers, and myriad plants thrive.

sentences.zh: 民何貴？

translations.literal: What is precious for the people?
translations.idiomatic: What do people prize most?

sentences.zh: 貴有德。

translations.literal: They prize having virtue.
translations.idiomatic: They prize virtue above all.

sentences.zh: 安其所，樂終產。

translations.literal: Secure them in their place, and delight reaches life's completion.
translations.idiomatic: Set people securely in their proper place, and they can live out life in peace.

sentences.zh: 樂終產，世繼緒。

translations.literal: If joy reaches life's completion, generations continue the line.
translations.idiomatic: When peace endures to life's end, the lineage continues across generations.

sentences.zh: 飛龍秋，游上天。

translations.literal: The flying dragon rises in autumn, roaming the heavens above.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn the flying dragon ascends and ranges through the upper sky.

sentences.zh: 高賢愉，樂民人。

translations.literal: The great worthy is serene, delighting the people.
translations.idiomatic: The great worthy is at ease and brings joy to the people.

sentences.zh: 豐草葽，女羅施。

translations.literal: Luxuriant grasses spread; dodder vines are laid out.
translations.idiomatic: Rich grasses grow thick, and trailing vines spread across them.

sentences.zh: 殒何如，誰能回！

translations.literal: How is ruin to be met? Who can turn it back?
translations.idiomatic: When collapse approaches, who can reverse it?

sentences.zh: 大莫大，成教德；

translations.literal: Nothing is greater than completing the virtue of teaching;
translations.idiomatic: Nothing surpasses bringing educational virtue to completion;

sentences.zh: 長莫長，被無極。

translations.literal: nothing is longer than extending it without limit.
translations.idiomatic: nothing endures longer than extending that virtue without bound.

sentences.zh: 雷震震，電燿燿。

translations.literal: Thunder crashes, lightning blazes.
translations.idiomatic: Thunder rumbles violently and lightning flashes bright.

sentences.zh: 明德鄉，治本約。

translations.literal: Facing toward manifest virtue, govern by keeping the root concise.
translations.idiomatic: Orient rule toward manifest virtue and keep governance rooted in essentials.

sentences.zh: 治本約，澤弘大。

translations.literal: When the root of governance is concise, beneficence grows vast.
translations.idiomatic: If governance is simple at the root, its benefits expand broadly.

sentences.zh: 加被寵，咸相保。

translations.literal: Bestow favor and protection, and all preserve one another.
translations.idiomatic: Granting favor and protection leads people to safeguard one another.

sentences.zh: 德施大，世曼壽。

translations.literal: When virtue is greatly applied, the age enjoys long life.
translations.idiomatic: When virtue is broadly enacted, the era enjoys prolonged flourishing.

sentences.zh: 都荔遂芳，窅窊桂華。

translations.literal: City chestnuts become fragrant; deep hollows bloom with cassia flowers.
translations.idiomatic: Fragrance fills city groves and cassia blossoms open in hidden valleys.

sentences.zh: 孝奏天儀，若日月光。

translations.literal: Filial devotion presents celestial rites, bright as sun and moon.
translations.idiomatic: Filial piety performs heaven's rites, shining like the sun and moon.

sentences.zh: 乘玄四龍，回馳北行。

translations.literal: Mounted on dark four dragons, they wheel and speed northward.
translations.idiomatic: Riding four dark dragons, the procession turns and races north.

sentences.zh: 羽旄殷盛，芬哉芒芒。

translations.literal: Plumed standards are richly abundant, fragrant and boundless.
translations.idiomatic: Feathered banners thicken in splendor, their fragrance vast and diffuse.

sentences.zh: 孝道隨世，我署文章。

translations.literal: The way of filiality follows the age; I inscribe these writings.
translations.idiomatic: As filial way adapts to the age, I set these lines into record.

sentences.zh: 桂華。

translations.literal: Cassia blossom.
translations.idiomatic: Cassia blossom.

sentences.zh: 馮馮翼翼，承天之則。

translations.literal: Vigorous and winglike, they receive Heaven's model.
translations.idiomatic: Grand and winged in bearing, they take Heaven's pattern as rule.

sentences.zh: 吾易久遠，燭明四極。

translations.literal: My transformation is long-enduring, illuminating the four extremities.
translations.idiomatic: The transformation I enact endures far and lights the world's four corners.

sentences.zh: 慈惠所愛，美若休德。

translations.literal: Compassionate kindness is cherished, beautiful like blessed virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Benevolent kindness is beloved, lovely as auspicious virtue.

sentences.zh: 杳杳冥冥，克綽永福。

translations.literal: Dim and deep, one can broadly secure lasting blessings.
translations.idiomatic: In deep hiddenness one can still secure enduring blessing.

sentences.zh: 美芳。

translations.literal: Beautiful fragrance.
translations.idiomatic: Fair fragrance.

sentences.zh: 磑磑即即，師象山則。

translations.literal: Resonant and ordered, taking mountains as model.
translations.idiomatic: Measured and resonant, modeled on mountain-like steadfastness.

sentences.zh: 烏呼孝哉，案撫戎國。

translations.literal: Ah, how filial! It pacifies and soothes frontier states.
translations.idiomatic: Ah, what filial power: it settles and comforts the border lands.

sentences.zh: 蠻夷竭歡，象來致福。

translations.literal: The southern and foreign peoples give full joy; elephants come bringing blessing.
translations.idiomatic: Outer peoples rejoice completely, and even elephants appear as auspicious tribute.

sentences.zh: 兼臨是愛，終無兵革。

translations.literal: Ruling all with this love, in the end there are no wars.
translations.idiomatic: Governing inclusively in this spirit, one ultimately leaves no room for war.

sentences.zh: 嘉薦芳矣，告靈饗矣。

translations.literal: A fine offering of fragrance is presented; we report that the spirits have partaken.
translations.idiomatic: Fragrant offerings are duly presented, and it is announced that the spirits have accepted them.

sentences.zh: 告靈既饗，德音孔臧。

translations.literal: Once the spirits have partaken, the sound of virtue is greatly excellent.
translations.idiomatic: When the spirits have received the rite, the voice of virtue rings supremely good.

sentences.zh: 惟德之臧，建侯之常。

translations.literal: Only when virtue is excellent is enfeoffing lords made constant.
translations.idiomatic: Only excellent virtue can sustain the enduring order of enfeoffing lords.

sentences.zh: 承保天休，令問不忘。

translations.literal: Receiving and preserving Heaven's favor, good repute is not forgotten.
translations.idiomatic: By preserving Heaven's blessing, honored reputation remains unforgettable.

sentences.zh: 皇皇鴻明，蕩侯休德。

translations.literal: August and vast in bright clarity, it stirs the lords' beneficent virtue.
translations.idiomatic: In sovereign brilliance it rouses the beneficent virtue of the feudal lords.

sentences.zh: 嘉承天和，伊樂厥福。

translations.literal: Well receiving Heaven's harmony, one thereby delights in its blessing.
translations.idiomatic: Receiving heavenly harmony well, one rejoices in the blessings it brings.

sentences.zh: 在樂不荒，惟民之則。

translations.literal: In joy there is no excess; this is the people's model.
translations.idiomatic: Joy without extravagance becomes the norm the people follow.

sentences.zh: 浚則師德，下民咸殖。

translations.literal: Deeply then one takes virtue as teacher, and all below prosper.
translations.idiomatic: Thus one takes virtue as master, and the people below all flourish.

sentences.zh: 令問在舊，孔容翼翼。

translations.literal: Good repute lies in ancient precedent; dignified bearing is reverent and ordered.
translations.idiomatic: Good reputation rests on inherited precedent, and dignified bearing remains disciplined.

sentences.zh: 孔容之常，承帝之明。

translations.literal: The constancy of dignified bearing receives the emperor's brilliance.
translations.idiomatic: Steady dignified conduct receives and reflects imperial radiance.

sentences.zh: 下民之樂，子孫保光。

translations.literal: The joy of the common people preserves glory for descendants.
translations.idiomatic: The people's contentment preserves brightness for future generations.

sentences.zh: 承順溫良，受帝之光。

translations.literal: By compliant warmth and goodness, one receives imperial light.
translations.idiomatic: Through gentle obedience and kindness, one receives the emperor's light.

sentences.zh: 嘉薦令芳，壽考不忘。

translations.literal: Fine offerings and fair fragrance: longevity is not forgotten.
translations.idiomatic: With worthy offerings and pure fragrance, prayers for long life are sustained.

sentences.zh: 承帝明德，師象山則。

translations.literal: Receiving imperial bright virtue, one takes mountain norms as model.
translations.idiomatic: Receiving the emperor's luminous virtue, one models oneself on mountain-like standards.

sentences.zh: 雲施稱民，永受厥福。

translations.literal: Cloudlike beneficence matching the people: forever receiving this blessing.
translations.idiomatic: Beneficence spreads like clouds among the people, and its blessing is received without end.

sentences.zh: 承容之常，承帝之明。

translations.literal: Receiving the constancy of bearing, receiving imperial brilliance.
translations.idiomatic: Receiving enduring propriety, one receives imperial radiance.

sentences.zh: 下民安樂，受福無疆。

translations.literal: The common people live in peace and joy, receiving boundless blessings.
translations.idiomatic: The populace lives securely and joyfully under limitless blessing.

sentences.zh: 郊祀歌十九章，其詩曰：

translations.literal: The suburban-sacrifice songs had nineteen sections; their verses say:
translations.idiomatic: The songs for suburban sacrifice were in nineteen parts, beginning as follows:

sentences.zh: 練時日，侯有望，巽膋蕭，延四方。

translations.literal: Select the right time and day; there is expectant watching; with fragrant fat and wormwood, extend to the four directions.
translations.idiomatic: Choose the proper day and hour, wait in reverence, offer fragrant fats and ritual herbs, and extend the rite to all four quarters.

sentences.zh: 九重開，靈之斿，垂惠恩，鴻祜休。

translations.literal: The nine gates open; the spirit's banner roams; it lets down grace and favor, and great blessings of fortune.
translations.idiomatic: As the ninefold gates open and the spirit-banner moves, grace descends with great auspicious blessing.

sentences.zh: 靈之車，結玄雲，駕飛龍，羽旄紛。

translations.literal: The spirit's chariot knots dark clouds, drives flying dragons, and feathered standards swirl.
translations.idiomatic: The spirit-chariot is yoked to dark clouds, drawn by flying dragons amid whirling plumes.

sentences.zh: 靈之下，若風馬，左倉龍，右白虎。

translations.literal: As the spirit descends, like wind-borne horses, with blue dragon at left and white tiger at right.
translations.idiomatic: When the spirit comes down, swift as wind-horses, azure dragon to the left and white tiger to the right.

sentences.zh: 靈之來，神哉沛，先以雨，般裔裔。

translations.literal: The spirit's arrival is divinely abundant; first comes rain, spreading wide and flowing.
translations.idiomatic: The spirit arrives in overwhelming numinous force; rain comes first, spreading in broad flowing abundance.

sentences.zh: 靈之至，慶陰陰，相放怫，震澹心。

translations.literal: When the spirit arrives, blessings gather darkly; radiance bursts forth and shakes the heart.
translations.idiomatic: As the spirit descends, omens thicken and sudden brilliance stirs the heart.

sentences.zh: 靈已坐，五音飭，虞至旦，承靈億。
