translations.literal: The spirit is seated; the five tones are ordered; the rite continues to dawn, receiving vast numinous grace.
translations.idiomatic: Once the spirit takes its seat, the five tones are set in order and worship proceeds until dawn in expectation of great blessing.

sentences.zh: 牲繭栗，粢盛香，尊桂酒，賓八鄉。

translations.literal: Victims are offered, grain fragrant, cassia wine raised, hosting the eight directions.
translations.idiomatic: Sacrificial beasts, fragrant grain, and cassia wine are presented to welcome powers from all directions.

sentences.zh: 靈安留，吟青黃，遍觀此，眺瑤堂。

translations.literal: May the spirit stay in peace, chanting blue and yellow, surveying all this and gazing on the jade hall.
translations.idiomatic: May the spirit linger in serenity, surveying the rite and gazing toward the jade hall.

sentences.zh: 眾嫭並，綽奇麗，顏如荼，兆逐靡。

translations.literal: Many beauties gather, graceful and strange in splendor, faces bright as blossoms, signs moving in procession.
translations.idiomatic: Attendants gather in brilliant beauty, their floral faces and moving formations filling the scene with auspicious display.

sentences.zh: 被華文，廁霧縠，曳阿錫，佩珠玉。

translations.literal: They wear ornate patterns, mixed with gauze like mist, trailing silk and wearing pearls and jade.
translations.idiomatic: Robed in patterned silk and gauze, they trail long sashes and wear pearl-and-jade ornaments.

sentences.zh: 俠嘉夜，仂蘭芳，澹容與，獻嘉觴。

translations.literal: In the fine night they labor with orchid fragrance, calm in bearing, offering blessed cups.
translations.idiomatic: In the auspicious night, amid orchid fragrance, they move with composure and present ceremonial cups.

sentences.zh: 練時日一

translations.literal: Selecting times and days, Song One.
translations.idiomatic: Song One: On choosing proper times and days.

sentences.zh: 帝臨中壇，四方承宇，繩繩意變，備得其所。

translations.literal: The emperor comes to the central altar; the four directions receive the ritual space; in ordered sequence all transformations find their place.
translations.idiomatic: At the central altar the emperor presides; all directions are encompassed and each ritual transformation is set in proper order.

sentences.zh: 清和六合，制數以五。

translations.literal: Pure harmony pervades the six directions; numbers are regulated by the five.
translations.idiomatic: Clear harmony fills the cosmos, with order measured through the fivefold numerological scheme.

sentences.zh: 海內安寧，興文匽武。

translations.literal: Within the seas there is peace; civil culture rises and warfare is restrained.
translations.idiomatic: The realm is at peace, civil governance flourishes, and martial force is checked.

sentences.zh: 后土富媼，昭明三光。

translations.literal: Houtu, rich and nurturing, illuminates the three lights.
translations.idiomatic: The Earth Mother, abundant and nourishing, reflects the brilliance of sun, moon, and stars.

sentences.zh: 穆穆優游，嘉服上黃。

translations.literal: Solemn and serene in easeful movement, in fine garments of supreme yellow.
translations.idiomatic: With grave composure they move at leisure, clothed in exalted yellow vestments.

sentences.zh: 帝臨二

translations.literal: The emperor arrives, Song Two.
translations.idiomatic: Song Two: The emperor's arrival.

sentences.zh: 青陽開動，根荄以遂，膏潤并愛，跂行畢逮。

translations.literal: Blue spring yang opens and moves; roots and shoots are fulfilled; rich moisture lovingly reaches all, even those that walk on tiptoe.
translations.idiomatic: When spring yang opens, root and shoot flourish; nourishing moisture extends to all beings.

sentences.zh: 霆聲發榮，壧處頃聽，枯槁復產，乃成厥命。

translations.literal: Thunder's sound brings forth growth; cliff places and low places alike hear; the withered produce again, thus fulfilling their mandate.
translations.idiomatic: Thunder awakens life everywhere, and even what was withered renews itself, fulfilling Heaven's charge.

sentences.zh: 眾庶熙熙，施及夭胎，群生啿啿，惟春之祺。

translations.literal: The multitude are joyful; grace reaches even unborn embryos; all creatures delight—this is spring's blessing.
translations.idiomatic: People rejoice, beneficence extends to the unborn, and all beings thrive: such is spring's auspicious gift.

sentences.zh: 青陽三鄒子樂。

translations.literal: Blue Spring, Song Three of Zouzi.
translations.idiomatic: "Blue Spring," the third Zouzi hymn.

sentences.zh: 朱明盛長，归與萬物，桐生茂豫，靡有所詘。

translations.literal: Vermilion brightness flourishes and grows, returning with all things; tung trees grow luxuriant with nothing bent or stunted.
translations.idiomatic: Summer's vermilion brilliance expands with all life; vegetation grows lush and uncurtailed.

sentences.zh: 敷華就實，既阜既昌，登成甫田，百鬼迪嘗。

translations.literal: Flowers spread and fruits form; abundance and prosperity rise; fields mature and the hundred spirits partake.
translations.idiomatic: Blossom becomes fruit, harvest fields ripen, and offerings are shared with the spirits.

sentences.zh: 廣大建祀，肅雍不忘，神若宥之，傳世無疆。

translations.literal: Great sacrifices are established broad and solemn; if the spirits grant favor, transmission through generations is without bound.
translations.idiomatic: Vast solemn rites are maintained, and with divine favor the lineage continues endlessly.

sentences.zh: 朱明四鄒子樂。

translations.literal: Vermilion Brightness, Song Four of Zouzi.
translations.idiomatic: "Vermilion Brightness," the fourth Zouzi hymn.

sentences.zh: 西顥沆碭，秋氣肅殺，含秀垂穎，續舊不廢。

translations.literal: White autumn vast and clear; autumn qi is austere and cutting; hidden beauty bears ears, continuing the old without abandonment.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn's clear severity, grain heads mature and established order continues unbroken.

sentences.zh: 姦偽不萌，祅孽伏息，隅辟越遠，四貉咸服。

translations.literal: Fraud and monstrosity do not sprout; deviant evils lie still; remote corners and distant Yue come to submission.
translations.idiomatic: Deceit and prodigy are suppressed, and distant regions submit to orderly rule.

sentences.zh: 既畏茲威，惟慕純德，附而不驕，正心翊翊。

translations.literal: Fearing this majesty, they admire pure virtue; they attach themselves without arrogance, setting their hearts upright in reverent aid.
translations.idiomatic: Awed by such authority, they are drawn to pure virtue, attaching themselves humbly and rectifying their hearts.

sentences.zh: 西顥五鄒子樂。

translations.literal: Western Hao, Song Five of Zouzi.
translations.idiomatic: "Western Brightness," the fifth Zouzi hymn.

sentences.zh: 玄冥陵陰，蟄蟲蓋臧，屮木零落，抵冬降霜。

translations.literal: Dark winter rises with yin; hibernating creatures are hidden; grasses and trees fall, and frost descends at winter's arrival.
translations.idiomatic: In winter's dark yin, creatures withdraw, vegetation withers, and frost settles over the land.

sentences.zh: 易亂除邪，革正異俗，兆民反本，抱素懷樸。

translations.literal: Transform disorder and remove perversity; reform and rectify deviant customs; the myriad people return to roots, embracing simplicity and plainness.
translations.idiomatic: Disorder is corrected, corrupt customs reformed, and the people return to elemental simplicity.

sentences.zh: 條理信義，望禮五嶽。

translations.literal: Order faith and righteousness, and look in ritual toward the Five Peaks.
translations.idiomatic: Set trust and righteousness in order, honoring the Five Sacred Peaks through ritual.

sentences.zh: 籍斂之時，掩收嘉穀。

translations.literal: At the time of tax levy, store up and gather fine grain.
translations.idiomatic: At harvest and levy season, good grain is gathered and stored.

sentences.zh: 玄冥六鄒子樂。

translations.literal: Dark Winter, Song Six of Zouzi.
translations.idiomatic: "Dark Winter," the sixth Zouzi hymn.

sentences.zh: 惟泰元尊，媼神蕃釐，經緯天地，作成四時。

translations.literal: Only the Great Origin is exalted; the nurturing deities bestow abundant blessings; they warp and weft Heaven and Earth and bring forth the four seasons.
translations.idiomatic: The Great Origin and nurturing spirits structure Heaven and Earth and generate the cycle of four seasons.

sentences.zh: 精建日月，星辰度理，陰陽五行，周而復始。

translations.literal: They precisely establish sun and moon; stars and constellations follow measure; yin-yang and five phases cycle and return.
translations.idiomatic: Sun, moon, stars, and the five-phase yin-yang order are set in measured cyclical recurrence.

sentences.zh: 雲風雷電，降甘露雨，百姓蕃滋，咸循厥緒。

translations.literal: Cloud, wind, thunder, and lightning send sweet dew and rain; the people flourish in number, all following their proper thread.
translations.idiomatic: Clouds, winds, thunder, and rain nourish growth, and the people multiply while following proper order.

sentences.zh: 繼統共勤，順皇之德，鸞路龍鱗，罔不肸飾。

translations.literal: Inheriting the line, all labor together, following imperial virtue; phoenix carriages and dragon scales are all splendidly adorned.
translations.idiomatic: In loyal succession all serve diligently under imperial virtue, and ceremonial vehicles glitter in ornate display.

sentences.zh: 嘉籩列陳，庶幾宴享，滅除凶災，列騰八荒。

translations.literal: Fine baskets are arrayed in rows for feast and offering, destroying ill omens and lifting auspicious force to the eight wastes.
translations.idiomatic: Sacrificial vessels are set out for banquet and offering, warding off calamity and spreading blessing to every horizon.

sentences.zh: 鐘鼓竽笙，雲舞翔翔，招搖靈旗，九夷賓將。

translations.literal: Bells, drums, reed-pipes, and sheng; cloud-dances soaring; spirit banners fluttering; the nine border peoples attend as guests.
translations.idiomatic: Bells and winds resound while cloud-dances rise; spirit banners stream and distant peoples come as guests.

sentences.zh: 惟泰元七建始元年，丞相匡衡奏罷「鸞路龍鱗」，更定詩曰「涓選休成」。

translations.literal: In the first year of Jianshi, Chancellor Kuang Heng memorialized abolishing "phoenix carriage and dragon scales" and revised the poem as "Carefully Selected Restful Completion."
translations.idiomatic: In Jianshi year one, Chancellor Kuang Heng petitioned to remove the phrase "phoenix carriage and dragon scales," replacing it with "carefully chosen, peacefully completed."

sentences.zh: 天地並況，惟予有慕，爰熙紫壇，思求厥路。

translations.literal: Heaven and Earth both manifest; I alone hold admiration; therefore I brighten the purple altar and seek its path.
translations.idiomatic: As Heaven and Earth reveal themselves, I approach the purple altar in reverence, seeking the proper ritual way.

sentences.zh: 恭承禋祀，縕豫為紛，黼繡周張，承神至尊。

translations.literal: Reverently bearing the incense sacrifice, with abundant layered adornment, patterned embroidery spread all around to receive the supreme spirit.
translations.idiomatic: With reverent incense offering and rich ritual adornment, embroidered vesture is spread to welcome the highest spirit.

sentences.zh: 千童羅舞成八溢，合好效歡虞泰一。

translations.literal: A thousand youths in ranked dance complete the eight rows, harmonizing delight to serve Taiyi.
translations.idiomatic: A thousand youths dance in ordered eight ranks, uniting in joyful service to Taiyi.

sentences.zh: 九歌畢奏斐然殊，鳴琴竽瑟會軒朱。

translations.literal: After the nine songs are fully played, exceptionally distinct, qin, yu, and se sound together in vermilion halls.
translations.idiomatic: When the nine hymns conclude, qin and zithers sound together through vermilion courts in splendid accord.

sentences.zh: 璆磬金鼓，靈其有喜，百官濟濟，各敬厥事。

translations.literal: Jade chimes, metal drums—perhaps the spirit is pleased; officials in full array each reverently attend to duty.
translations.idiomatic: Jade chimes and bronze drums resound; if the spirit is pleased, all officials perform their duties with reverence.

sentences.zh: 盛牲實俎進聞膏，神奄留，臨須搖。

translations.literal: Great victims fill the stands and rich fat is presented; the spirit lingers briefly, facing and swaying.
translations.idiomatic: Substantial offerings are brought forward; the spirit seems to pause, present and responsive.

sentences.zh: 長麗前掞光燿明，寒暑不忒況皇章。

translations.literal: Long radiance flares before us in brilliant light; cold and heat do not deviate, displaying imperial pattern.
translations.idiomatic: A long brilliant glow appears; seasons keep true measure, manifesting imperial order.

sentences.zh: 展詩應律鋗玉鳴，函宮吐角激徵清。

translations.literal: Unfurling verses to match pitch, jade pendants ring; palace tone contains and horn tone emerges, striking clear zhi.
translations.idiomatic: Verses align with pitch as jade pendants chime; tones unfold in ordered clarity.

sentences.zh: 發梁揚羽申以商，造茲新音永久長。

translations.literal: Raise gong-beams and lift yu, extending with shang; creating this new music, enduring forever long.
translations.idiomatic: Gong and yu are raised, shang extended, forming new music meant to endure without end.

sentences.zh: 聲氣遠條鳳鳥启，神夕奄虞蓋孔享。

translations.literal: Breath and sound stretch far; phoenix birds open; at dusk the spirit still enjoys and is greatly feasted.
translations.idiomatic: Sound and vital breath travel afar; phoenix omens appear, and the spirit is richly satisfied.

sentences.zh: 天地八丞相匡衡奏罷「黼繡周張」，更定詩曰「肅若舊典」。

translations.literal: Song Eight of Heaven and Earth: Chancellor Kuang Heng memorialized dropping "patterned embroidery spread all around" and revised the line as "solemn as ancient canon."
translations.idiomatic: In Song Eight, Kuang Heng replaced "embroidery spread all around" with "solemn as the old canon."

sentences.zh: 日出入安窮？

translations.literal: How can sunrise and sunset be exhausted?
translations.idiomatic: How could the cycle of sunrise and sunset ever be exhausted?

sentences.zh: 時世不與人同。

translations.literal: Times and ages are not the same as persons.
translations.idiomatic: Historical ages do not move in step with any one person.

sentences.zh: 故春非我春，夏非我夏，秋非我秋，冬非我冬。

translations.literal: Therefore spring is not my spring, summer not my summer, autumn not my autumn, winter not my winter.
translations.idiomatic: So the seasons are no one person's possession: spring, summer, autumn, and winter are not mine alone.

sentences.zh: 泊如四海之池，遍觀是邪謂何？

translations.literal: Still as the pool of the four seas—what is this I see everywhere?
translations.idiomatic: Calm as the pool of the four seas, I ask what this pervasive vision signifies.

sentences.zh: 吾知所樂，獨樂六龍，六龍之調，使我心若。

translations.literal: I know what I delight in: I delight only in six dragons; the tuning of six dragons makes my heart compliant.
translations.idiomatic: I know my joy: only the six dragons' harmony, whose tuning sets my heart at rest.

sentences.zh: 訾黃其何不徠下！

translations.literal: Why does yellow radiance not come down?
translations.idiomatic: Why does the yellow radiance not descend?

sentences.zh: 日出入九

translations.literal: Sunrise-Sunset, Song Nine.
translations.idiomatic: "Sunrise-Sunset," ninth song.

sentences.zh: 太一況，天馬下，霑赤汗，沬流赭。

translations.literal: At Taiyi's manifestation, heavenly horses descend, drenched in red sweat, foaming dark-red streams.
translations.idiomatic: When Taiyi appears, heavenly horses descend, sweating blood-red and foaming crimson.

sentences.zh: 志俶儻，精權奇，逦浮雲，晻上馳。

translations.literal: Their will is bold and unbridled, their spirit extraordinary; trailing floating clouds, dimly they race upward.
translations.idiomatic: Bold in spirit and strange in power, they trail cloud-banks as they surge upward.

sentences.zh: 體容與，迣萬里，今安匹，龍為友。

translations.literal: Their bodies are poised and free, coursing ten thousand li; who can match them now? Dragons are their companions.
translations.idiomatic: Their bearing is effortless as they roam immense distances; none can match them, for dragons are their peers.

sentences.zh: 元狩三年馬生渥洼水中作。

translations.literal: Composed in Yuanshou year three when a horse was born in Wuwa waters.
translations.idiomatic: Composed in Yuanshou 3 on the appearance of a heavenly horse at Wuwa waters.

sentences.zh: 天馬徠，從西極，涉流沙，九夷服。

translations.literal: Heavenly horses come from the western extreme, crossing shifting sands, and the nine frontier peoples submit.
translations.idiomatic: The heavenly horse comes from the far west across the sands, and distant peoples submit.

sentences.zh: 天馬徠，出泉水，虎脊兩，化若鬼。

translations.literal: Heavenly horses come, emerging from spring waters, with tiger-like double backs, transforming like spirits.
translations.idiomatic: The heavenly horse emerges from springs, tiger-backed and uncanny in spirit-like transformation.

sentences.zh: 天馬徠，歷無草，徑千里，循東道。

translations.literal: Heavenly horses come, traversing grassless tracts, going straight a thousand li, following the eastern road.
translations.idiomatic: The heavenly horse crosses barren expanses, running a thousand li along the eastern route.

sentences.zh: 天馬徠，執徐時，將搖舉，誰與期？

translations.literal: Heavenly horses come in the zhi-xu season; about to rise and soar—who can keep the appointment?
translations.idiomatic: The heavenly horse arrives at the appointed season, ready to soar; who can truly answer that summons?

sentences.zh: 天馬徠，開遠門，竦予身，逝昆侖。

translations.literal: Heavenly horses come, opening distant gates, lifting my body, departing to Kunlun.
translations.idiomatic: The heavenly horse opens far gates, lifting me as if toward Kunlun.

sentences.zh: 天馬徠，龍之媒，游閶闔，觀玉臺。

translations.literal: Heavenly horses come, companions of dragons, roaming Changhe and viewing the jade terrace.
translations.idiomatic: The heavenly horse, mate to dragons, roams celestial gates and gazes on jade terraces.

sentences.zh: 太初四年誅宛王獲宛馬作。

translations.literal: Composed in Taichu year four after executing the king of Dayuan and obtaining Dayuan horses.
translations.idiomatic: Composed in Taichu 4 after defeating Dayuan and obtaining Ferghana horses.

sentences.zh: 天馬十

translations.literal: Heavenly Horse, Song Ten.
translations.idiomatic: "Heavenly Horse," tenth song.

sentences.zh: 天門開，詄蕩蕩，穆並騁，以臨饗。

translations.literal: Heaven's gate opens vast and unbound; solemnly they race together to approach the feast.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's gate opens in sweeping vastness as the procession advances to the sacred feast.

sentences.zh: 光夜燭，德信著，靈娅平而鴻，長生豫。

translations.literal: Light illumines the night; virtue and trust are manifest; the spirit is pacified and grand, and long life is joyful.
translations.idiomatic: Night is lit with radiance, virtue and fidelity made visible; the numinous order is settled and life prolonged in joy.

sentences.zh: 大朱涂廣，夷石為堂，飾玉梢以舞歌，體招搖若永望。

translations.literal: Great vermilion roads are broad; leveled stones make halls; jade tassels adorn dance and song; the body sways in lasting gaze.
translations.idiomatic: Broad vermilion avenues and stone halls are adorned with jade pendants, while dance and song sway in enduring spectacle.

sentences.zh: 星留俞，塞隕光，照紫幄，珠熉黃。

translations.literal: Stars linger and return, blocked light falls, shining on purple canopies, pearls glowing yellow.
translations.idiomatic: Stars pause and wheel, light falls across purple canopies, and pearls gleam golden.

sentences.zh: 幡比还集，貳雙飛常羊。

translations.literal: Banners gather side by side; paired forms fly and wander.
translations.idiomatic: Banners converge in ranks while paired figures wheel in flight-like motion.

sentences.zh: 月穆穆以金波，日華燿以宣明。

translations.literal: The moon is solemn with golden waves; the sun's splendor blazes in full brightness.
translations.idiomatic: The moon moves with golden sheen and the sun pours out brilliant light.

sentences.zh: 假清風軋忽，激長至重觴。

translations.literal: Borrowing pure wind in sudden turns, they stir long continuance with repeated cups.
translations.idiomatic: Pure wind sweeps suddenly, enlivening the long rite as cups are offered again.

sentences.zh: 神裴回若留放，殣冀親以肆章。

translations.literal: The spirit circles as if lingering and releasing; with urgent hope we draw near and unfold the hymn.
translations.idiomatic: The spirit circles as though lingering; in eager hope we approach and extend the ceremonial ode.

sentences.zh: 函蒙祉福常若期，寂漻上天知厥時。

translations.literal: Enclosing bestowed blessings always as promised, high heaven in still vastness knows its time.
translations.idiomatic: Blessings are held as once promised, while lofty heaven in stillness knows the appointed hour.

sentences.zh: 泛泛滇滇從高斿，殷勤此路臚所求。

translations.literal: Vastly and broadly following high banners, earnestly this road declares what is sought.
translations.idiomatic: Following lofty banners over broad space, this ceremonial path faithfully displays its intent.

sentences.zh: 佻正嘉吉弘以昌，休嘉砰隱溢四方。

translations.literal: Upright auspicious fortune expands in flourishing; excellent blessing overflows the four directions.
translations.idiomatic: Auspicious rectitude grows and prosperous blessing spills across all quarters.

sentences.zh: 專精厲意逝九閡，紛云六幕浮大海。

translations.literal: With focused will and sharpened purpose we pass the nine barriers; clouds spread through six curtains and float over the great sea.
translations.idiomatic: With concentrated intent we traverse the nine passes as cloud-masses drift across the six veils over the great sea.

sentences.zh: 天門十一

translations.literal: Heaven's Gate, Song Eleven.
translations.idiomatic: "Heaven's Gate," eleventh song.

sentences.zh: 景星顯見，信星彪列，象載昭庭，日親以察。

translations.literal: A bright omen star appears; faithful stars line in ranks; images are carried to the shining court, and each day we draw near to inspect.
translations.idiomatic: Auspicious stars appear in ordered array and are presented before the radiant court for close observance.

sentences.zh: 參侔開闔，爰推本紀，汾脽出鼎，皇祜元始。

translations.literal: Matching openings and closings in triads, we trace foundational records; at Fenyin a cauldron emerged, imperial blessing beginning at origin.
translations.idiomatic: By cosmological correspondences we read foundational omens: the Fenyin tripod appeared as a sign of primal imperial blessing.

sentences.zh: 五音六律，依韋饗昭，雜變並會，雅聲遠姚。

translations.literal: Five tones and six pitches, depending on tanned-hide ceremony are made manifest; mixed transformations converge, and elegant sound extends far.
translations.idiomatic: Five tones and six pitch-standards are ritually aligned; varied musical transformations converge, sending refined sound afar.

sentences.zh: 空桑琴瑟結信成，四興遞代八風生。

translations.literal: Kongsang qin and zithers join in faithful completion; four excitations alternate and the eight winds arise.
translations.idiomatic: Qin and zither from Kongsang unite in concord; four modal impulses alternate and the eight winds are stirred.

sentences.zh: 殷殷鐘石羽籥鳴。

translations.literal: Resounding, resounding, bells and stone chimes, with feather-flutes sounding.
translations.idiomatic: Bells, lithophones, and feather flutes resound in heavy rhythmic fullness.

sentences.zh: 河龍供鯉醇犧牲。

translations.literal: River dragons offer carp and rich sacrificial victims.
translations.idiomatic: River-dragon omens present carp and rich sacrificial offerings.

sentences.zh: 百末旨酒布蘭生。

translations.literal: A hundred blended fine wines are spread; orchid fragrance arises.
translations.idiomatic: Many refined wines are arrayed, and orchid fragrance rises through the rite.

sentences.zh: 泰尊柘漿析朝酲。

translations.literal: From the great honored vessel, mulberry brew dispels morning intoxication.
translations.idiomatic: From the great ritual cup, mulberry liquor clears the lingering morning haze.

sentences.zh: 微感心攸通修名，周流常羊思所并。

translations.literal: Subtle resonance reaches the heart and cultivates reputation; circling and flowing, thought joins what it seeks.
translations.idiomatic: Subtle response penetrates the heart, and as thought circulates it unites with its intended object.

sentences.zh: 穰穰復正直往甯，馮蠵切和疏寫平。

translations.literal: Abundant abundance returns to upright peace; in close harmony roughness is loosened and balance made plain.
translations.idiomatic: Rich plenty returns under rectitude; through harmonizing discipline, harshness is softened into equilibrium.

sentences.zh: 上天布施后土成，穰穰豐年四時榮。

translations.literal: High heaven bestows and earth completes; abundant harvest years glorify the four seasons.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven dispenses and Earth fulfills, yielding rich harvest years and seasonal prosperity.

sentences.zh: 景星十二元鼎五年得鼎汾陰作。

translations.literal: Auspicious Star, Song Twelve, composed in Yuanding year five when a cauldron was found at Fenyin.
translations.idiomatic: "Auspicious Star," twelfth song, composed in Yuanding 5 after the Fenyin tripod omen.

sentences.zh: 齊房產草，九莖連葉，宮童效異，披圖案諜。

translations.literal: In the fasting chamber grass appeared, nine stems with joined leaves; palace youths imitated this marvel, consulting charts and records.
translations.idiomatic: In the ritual chamber a nine-stemmed plant appeared; palace attendants verified the marvel against charts and records.

sentences.zh: 玄氣之精，回復此都，蔓蔓日茂，芝成靈華。

translations.literal: The essence of dark qi returned to this capital; spreading day by day luxuriant, the zhi became numinous blossom.
translations.idiomatic: The subtle essence of dark vital force returned to the capital, where sacred fungus flourished into spiritual bloom.

sentences.zh: 齊房十三元封二年芝生甘泉齊房作。

translations.literal: Fasting Chamber, Song Thirteen, composed in Yuanfeng year two when zhi grew in Ganquan fasting chamber.
translations.idiomatic: "Fasting Chamber," thirteenth song, composed in Yuanfeng 2 after numinous fungus appeared at Ganquan.

sentences.zh: 后皇嘉壇，立玄黃服，物發冀州，兆蒙祉福。

translations.literal: At the splendid altar of the Sovereign Earth, black-and-yellow robes are established; things arise in Jizhou, and signs receive blessed fortune.
translations.idiomatic: At the august earth altar, black-and-yellow vestments are donned, and omens of blessing arise across the realm.

sentences.zh: 沇沇四塞，徦狄合處，經營萬億，咸遂厥宇。

translations.literal: Flowing on all four borders, distant peoples gather; managing ten-thousandfold affairs, all fulfill their dwellings.
translations.idiomatic: Waters and peoples converge from all borders; under vast administration, all communities secure their proper place.

sentences.zh: 后皇十四

translations.literal: Sovereign Earth, Song Fourteen.
translations.idiomatic: "Sovereign Earth," fourteenth song.

sentences.zh: 華鳞鳞，固靈根。

translations.literal: Brilliant scales gleam, firming the numinous root.
translations.idiomatic: Shining scales glitter, confirming the strength of the sacred root.

sentences.zh: 神之斿，過天門，車千乘，敦昆侖。

translations.literal: The spirit's banner passes Heaven's gate; chariots by the thousand ascend Kunlun.
translations.idiomatic: The spirit-procession passes the celestial gate, with a thousand chariots mounting toward Kunlun.

sentences.zh: 神之出，排玉房，周流雜，拔蘭堂。

translations.literal: As the spirit comes forth, it opens jade chambers, circling through and drawing up from orchid halls.
translations.idiomatic: When the spirit emerges, it sweeps through jeweled halls and rises from fragrant orchid chambers.

sentences.zh: 神之行，旌容容，騎沓沓，般縱縱。

translations.literal: As the spirit moves, banners billow broad; riders crowd close, circling in ordered array.
translations.idiomatic: In the spirit's advance, standards ripple and mounted ranks wheel in dense ceremonial motion.

sentences.zh: 神之徠，泛翊翊，甘露降，慶雲集。

translations.literal: When the spirit arrives, radiance spreads in wings; sweet dew descends and auspicious clouds gather.
translations.idiomatic: At the spirit's coming, brightness unfolds, sweet dew falls, and celebratory clouds assemble.

sentences.zh: 神之揄，臨壇宇，九疑賓，夔龍舞。

translations.literal: When the spirit soars and nears the altar precinct, Jiuyi attends as guest, and Kui with dragons dance.
translations.idiomatic: As the spirit approaches the altar, distant realms appear in homage, and mythic ministers dance.

sentences.zh: 神安坐，启吉時，共翊翊，合所思。

translations.literal: The spirit sits in peace, opening auspicious time; all assist together, fulfilling what was intended.
translations.idiomatic: The spirit settles into its seat, inaugurating a fortunate hour as all participants realize the rite's intent.

sentences.zh: 神嘉虞，申貳觴，福滂洋，邁延長。

translations.literal: The spirit delights in the rite; second cups are offered; blessings pour vast and life is extended long.
translations.idiomatic: As offerings continue, blessing flows abundantly, promising extended life.

sentences.zh: 沛施祐，汾之阿，揚金光，橫泰河，莽若雲，增陽波。

translations.literal: Greatly it bestows aid by the bend of Fenyin, raising golden light across the great river, vast as clouds, increasing sunlit waves.
translations.idiomatic: At Fenyin's river bend, divine favor surges like cloud-masses, casting golden light across broad waters.

sentences.zh: 遍臚驩，騰天歌。

translations.literal: Joy is proclaimed everywhere, and heavenly song rises.
translations.idiomatic: Rejoicing is announced throughout the realm, and hymns ascend skyward.

sentences.zh: 華鳞鳞十五

translations.literal: Brilliant Scales, Song Fifteen.
translations.idiomatic: 'Brilliant Scales,' fifteenth song.

sentences.zh: 五神相，包四鄰，土地廣，揚浮雲。

translations.literal: The five spirits correspond, encompassing the four borders; lands are broad and floating clouds rise.
translations.idiomatic: The five spiritual powers align across all frontiers, over expansive lands and lifting clouds.

sentences.zh: 扢嘉壇，椒蘭芳，璧玉精，垂華光。

translations.literal: At the splendid altar, pepper and orchid are fragrant; jade disks are pure, hanging in radiant light.
translations.idiomatic: At the auspicious altar, aromatic offerings and polished jade glow in ceremonial brilliance.

sentences.zh: 益億年，美始興，交於神，若有承。

translations.literal: Adding ten thousand years, beauty newly arises; joining with the spirits, as though receiving mandate.
translations.idiomatic: May years be extended without end as renewed splendor joins the spirits under heavenly sanction.

sentences.zh: 廣宣延，咸畢觴，靈輿位，偃蹇驤。

translations.literal: Broadly proclaimed and prolonged, all cups are completed; the spirit carriage takes its place, lofty and soaring.
translations.idiomatic: Proclamation extends widely, libations are completed, and the spirit-car stands poised in exalted motion.

sentences.zh: 卉汨臚，析奚道？

translations.literal: Flourishing abundance spreads-how can one fully explain its way?
translations.idiomatic: Prosperity overflows, beyond easy explanation.

sentences.zh: 淫淥澤，鬓然歸。

translations.literal: Luxuriant green moisture, and then in full array it returns.
translations.idiomatic: Rich green vitality gathers and returns in complete form.

sentences.zh: 五神十六

translations.literal: Five Spirits, Song Sixteen.
translations.idiomatic: 'Five Spirits,' sixteenth song.

sentences.zh: 朝隴首，覽西垠，剨電抠，獲白麟。

translations.literal: Facing Longshou, surveying western bounds, lightning flashes and one captures a white unicorn.
translations.idiomatic: At Longshou, after surveying the western frontier, lightning-like omens appeared and a white qilin was obtained.

sentences.zh: 爰五止，顯黃德，圖匈虐，熏鬻殛。

translations.literal: Thus the five halts displayed yellow virtue, plotting against Xiongnu violence and striking Xunyu tribes.
translations.idiomatic: These events manifested imperial yellow virtue and marked suppression of violent steppe powers.

sentences.zh: 闢流離，抑不詳，賓百僚，山河饗。

translations.literal: Driving out displacement, restraining disorder, the hundred officials are hosted and mountains and rivers partake.
translations.idiomatic: With turmoil quelled, officials are assembled in ritual order and the landscape itself is sacralized in offering.

sentences.zh: 掩回轅，鬗長馳，騰雨師，洒路陂。

translations.literal: Covering and turning chariot-poles, racing long and swift, summoning rain masters, sprinkling roads and banks.
translations.idiomatic: Chariot processions turned and sped; rain-invoking powers were summoned to moisten roads and embankments.

sentences.zh: 流星隕，感惟風，钆歸雲，撫懷心。

translations.literal: Meteors fell, feelings stirred by wind; metal and stone returned to cloud, touching the heart within.
translations.idiomatic: Falling stars and moving winds stirred deep emotion as omens folded back into the clouds.

sentences.zh: 朝隴首十七元狩元年行幸雍獲白麟作。

translations.literal: Facing Longshou, Song Seventeen, composed in Yuanshou year one when traveling to Yong and obtaining a white unicorn.
translations.idiomatic: 'Facing Longshou,' seventeenth song, composed in Yuanshou 1 after the Yong tour and capture of a white qilin.

sentences.zh: 象載瑜，白集西，食甘露，飲榮泉。

translations.literal: Elephant insignia bears shining jade; white gathers in the west; feeding on sweet dew and drinking glorious springs.
translations.idiomatic: The auspicious emblem gleams like jade in the west, nourished by dew and sacred springs.

sentences.zh: 赤鴈集，六紛員，殊翁雜，五采文。

translations.literal: Red geese gather; sixfold circles crowd; rare elders mingle, in five-colored pattern.
translations.idiomatic: Red geese assembled in auspicious formations amid multicolored omens and unusual signs.

sentences.zh: 神所見，施祉福，登蓬萊，結無極。

translations.literal: Seen by the spirits, blessings are bestowed; ascending Penglai, bound to the limitless.
translations.idiomatic: Under spiritual witness, blessings descend, linking ascent to Penglai with boundless destiny.

sentences.zh: 象載瑜十八太始三年行幸東海獲赤鴈作。

translations.literal: Elephant Carries Jade, Song Eighteen, composed in Taishi year three when touring East Sea and obtaining red geese.
translations.idiomatic: 'Elephant Carries Jade,' eighteenth song, composed in Taishi 3 after the Eastern Sea progress and red-goose omen.

sentences.zh: 赤蛟綏，黃華蓋，露夜零，晝晻濭。

translations.literal: Red dragons are at ease; yellow canopies bloom; dew falls at night, by day shadows gather.
translations.idiomatic: Red-dragon omens appeared beneath yellow canopies; nocturnal dew and dim daylight marked the rite.

sentences.zh: 百君禮，六龍位，勺椒漿，靈已醉。

translations.literal: Hundred lords perform rite, six dragons in position, ladling peppered wine, the spirit already intoxicated.
translations.idiomatic: With assembled lords in ceremony and six dragons arrayed, spiced libations were offered as the spirit was delighted.

sentences.zh: 靈既享，錫吉祥，芒芒極，降嘉觴。

translations.literal: The spirit having partaken grants auspiciousness; to the farthest reaches fine cups descend.
translations.idiomatic: After receiving the offerings, the spirit bestowed auspicious blessing to the realm's utmost limits.

sentences.zh: 靈殷殷，爛揚光，延壽命，永未央。

translations.literal: The spirit resplendent, radiantly spreading light, extends lifespan forever without end.
translations.idiomatic: Radiant divine presence shines forth, extending life and enduring prosperity.

sentences.zh: 杳冥冥，塞六合，澤汪濊，輯萬國。

translations.literal: Deeply dark and vast, filling the six directions; beneficence immense and abundant, gathering ten thousand states.
translations.idiomatic: In profound cosmic vastness, overflowing grace brings all polities into ordered union.

sentences.zh: 靈禗禗，象輿轙，票然逝，旗逶蛇。

translations.literal: The spirit in contemplation, with carved carriage yokes, swiftly departs, banners winding long.
translations.idiomatic: The spirit, borne in ceremonial carriage, departed swiftly while banners streamed in winding lines.

sentences.zh: 禮樂成，靈將歸，託玄德，長無衰。

translations.literal: Rites and music complete, the spirit will return; entrusted to dark virtue, long without decline.
translations.idiomatic: With ritual and music fulfilled, the spirit returns, leaving enduring mystery-virtue that does not fade.

sentences.zh: 赤蛟十九

translations.literal: Red Dragon, Song Nineteen.
translations.idiomatic: 'Red Dragon,' nineteenth song.

sentences.zh: 其餘巡狩福應之事，不序郊廟，故弗論。

translations.literal: As for other tour and omen events, they are not in suburban-temple sequence, therefore not discussed.
translations.idiomatic: Other omen events from imperial progresses fall outside the suburban-temple sequence and are omitted.

sentences.zh: 是時，河間獻王有雅材，亦以為治道非禮樂不成，因獻所集雅樂。

translations.literal: At that time the King Xian of Hejian had refined talent and also held that governance cannot succeed without ritual and music, so he presented the elegant music he had collected.
translations.idiomatic: The Prince Xian of Hejian believed effective rule required rites and music, and submitted his compiled orthodox repertory.

sentences.zh: 天子下大樂官，常存肄之，歲時以備數，然不常御，常御及郊廟皆非雅聲。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven sent it to the Grand Music Office, where it was regularly preserved and rehearsed for periodic use; yet it was not regularly performed, and what was regularly used, including at suburban and temple rites, was not elegant sound.
translations.idiomatic: The court archived and rehearsed it in the Grand Music Office, but standard performances-even in state sacrifices-still relied on non-orthodox styles.

sentences.zh: 然詩樂施於後嗣，猶得有所祖述。

translations.literal: Yet since poetic music was transmitted to later generations, there was still something to continue from ancestors.
translations.idiomatic: Because the poetic-musical tradition survived into later generations, a line of inheritance remained.

sentences.zh: 昔殷周之雅頌，乃上本有娀、姜原，镨、稷始生，玄王、公劉、古公、大伯、王季、姜女、大任、太姒之德，乃及成湯、文、武受命，武丁、成、康、宣王中興，下及輔佐阿衡、周、召、太公、申伯、召虎、仲山甫之屬，君臣男女有功德者，靡不褒揚。

translations.literal: In former times the Ya and Song of Yin and Zhou ultimately traced upward to Yousong and Jiangyuan, to the births of Xie and Ji, to the virtues of Xuanwang, Gongliu, Gugong, Taibo, Wangji, Jiangnu, Tairen, and Taisi; then to Cheng Tang and Kings Wen and Wu receiving mandate, to Wuding and Kings Cheng, Kang, and Xuan restoring prosperity; and downward to assisting ministers like A Heng, Zhou Gong, Shao Gong, Taigong, Shenbo, Shaohu, and Zhongshan Fu-all rulers, ministers, men and women with merit and virtue were without exception praised.
translations.idiomatic: The orthodox hymn tradition praised an unbroken line of founders, sage rulers, consorts, and ministers-from proto-ancestral figures through Shang and Zhou monarchs and their great aides-celebrating all whose virtue or achievement shaped the polity.

sentences.zh: 功德既信美矣，褒揚之聲盈乎天地之間，是以光名著於當世，遺譽垂於無窮也。

translations.literal: Their merits and virtues being truly excellent, the sounds of praise filled heaven and earth; thus their bright names were manifest in their own age, and remaining fame hung down without end.
translations.idiomatic: Because their achievements were genuinely noble, songs of praise resounded across the world, winning enduring renown for both their time and posterity.

sentences.zh: 今漢郊廟詩歌，未有祖宗之事，八音調均，又不協於鐘律，而內有掖庭材人，外有上林樂府，皆以鄭聲施於朝廷。

translations.literal: Now Han suburban and temple songs do not yet contain deeds of ancestral founders; though the eight sounds are evenly tuned, they do not accord with bell-pitch standards, and inside there are court entertainers and outside the Shanglin Music Bureau-all employing Zheng-style sounds in court.
translations.idiomatic: Current Han liturgical songs lack substantial ancestral narrative, and despite technical tuning they diverge from proper pitch law; court performance is dominated by Zheng-style popular sound.

sentences.zh: 至成帝時，謁者常山王禹世受可間樂，能說其義，其弟子宋嘱等上書言之，下大夫博士平當等考試。

translations.literal: By Emperor Cheng's time, the Court Gentleman Wang Yu of Changshan had inherited Kejian music and could explain its meaning; his disciples Song Zhu and others submitted a memorial, and learned officials such as Grandee Academician Ping Dang were ordered to examine it.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Cheng, Wang Yu transmitted an older musical line and his students petitioned for recognition, prompting formal scholarly review.

sentences.zh: 當以為「漢承秦滅道之後，賴先帝聖德，博受兼聽，修廢官，立大學，河間獻王聘求幽隱，修興雅樂以助化。

translations.literal: Dang considered: 'After Han inherited the destruction of the Way under Qin, relying on former emperors' sage virtue, broadly receiving and listening, restoring abolished offices, establishing the Imperial Academy, and with the King Xian of Hejian seeking out recluses to revive elegant music to aid transformation.'
translations.idiomatic: Ping Dang argued that Han, after Qin's devastation, had restored institutions and learning, and that Hejian's efforts to recover orthodox music should be seen as part of moral governance.

sentences.zh: 時，大儒公孫弘、董仲舒等皆以為音中正雅，立之大樂。

translations.literal: At that time great scholars such as Gongsun Hong and Dong Zhongshu all held that its sounds were centered, correct, and elegant, and established it in the Grand Music Office.
translations.idiomatic: Leading scholars like Gongsun Hong and Dong Zhongshu judged this repertory balanced and orthodox, and installed it in the Grand Music Office.

sentences.zh: 春秋鄉射，作於學官，希闊不講。

translations.literal: Spring and autumn archery rites were performed in school offices, but rarely and infrequently taught.
translations.idiomatic: Although seasonal archery rites existed in educational institutions, instruction became sparse and irregular.

sentences.zh: 故自公卿大夫觀聽者，但聞鑑鎗，不曉其意，而欲以風諭眾庶，其道無由。

translations.literal: Therefore from dukes and ministers to grandees who observed and listened, they only heard clanging sounds without understanding meaning; and wishing to use this to instruct the masses had no feasible path.
translations.idiomatic: Even high officials heard only sonic effect, not ritual meaning, so music could not function as moral instruction for the populace.

sentences.zh: 是以行之百有餘年，德化至今未成。

translations.literal: Thus though practiced for more than a hundred years, moral transformation to this day has not been completed.
translations.idiomatic: Hence after more than a century of practice, true ethical transformation remained unfinished.

sentences.zh: 今嘱等守習孤學，大指歸於興助教化。

translations.literal: Now Zhu and others preserve and study this isolated learning, whose main aim returns to promoting and assisting moral education.
translations.idiomatic: Song Zhu's group continued this marginalized tradition with the explicit aim of strengthening moral instruction.

sentences.zh: 衰微之學，興廢在人。

translations.literal: For learning in decline, its rise or fall depends on people.
translations.idiomatic: When a teaching grows weak, its survival depends on human commitment.

sentences.zh: 宜領屬雅樂，以繼絕表微。

translations.literal: It is fitting to lead and affiliate elegant music, so as to continue what is cut off and manifest what is subtle.
translations.idiomatic: Orthodox music should be officially led and integrated, to revive broken transmission and make subtle principles visible.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：『人能弘道，非道弘人。』

translations.literal: Confucius said: 'People can broaden the Way; it is not the Way that broadens people.'
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said, 'It is people who can enlarge the Way, not the Way that enlarges people.'

sentences.zh: 河間區區，不國藩臣，以好學修古，能有所存，民到于今稱之，況於聖主廣被之資，修起舊文，放鄭近雅，述而不作，信而好古，於以風示海內，揚名後世，誠非小功小美也。」

translations.literal: Hejian, though modest and not a central-state feudal lord, through love of learning and restoration of antiquity could preserve something, and people praise him to this day; how much more, then, with a sage ruler's broad endowment, could one restore old writings, reject Zheng and approach Ya, transmit without creating, trust and love antiquity, thereby instruct the realm and spread name to later ages-truly no small merit or beauty.'
translations.idiomatic: Though only a peripheral prince, Hejian won lasting praise by preserving ancient learning; under a true sage sovereign, restoring orthodox classics and moving away from Zheng-style sound could educate the whole realm and yield immense long-term merit.

sentences.zh: 事下公卿，以為久遠難分明，當議復寢。

translations.literal: The matter was sent down to dukes and ministers, who held that because of long time-span it was hard to determine clearly, and proposed to shelve discussion again.
translations.idiomatic: The proposal went to senior ministers, who judged the evidence too remote to settle decisively and moved to suspend it.

sentences.zh: 今海內更始，民人歸本，戶口歲息，平其刑辟，牧以賢良，至於家給，既庶且富，則須庠序禮樂之教化矣。

translations.literal: Now the realm has renewed; people return to roots; households grow year by year; punishments are leveled; governance uses the worthy and good; households are supplied, numerous and wealthy-therefore what is needed is educational transformation through schools, rites, and music.
translations.idiomatic: Now that the realm is stabilized, populous, and relatively prosperous under moderated law and capable officials, the next necessity is institutional moral education through schools, rites, and music.

sentences.zh: 今幸有前聖遺制之威儀，誠可法象而補備之，經紀可因緣而存著也。

translations.literal: Now fortunately there remain dignified forms left by former sages, which can truly be modeled on and supplemented to completeness; institutional frameworks can by these connections be preserved and made manifest.
translations.idiomatic: Because inherited sage-era ritual forms still survive, they can be taken as models, completed, and used to preserve coherent institutions.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：「殷因於夏禮，所損益，可知也；

translations.literal: Confucius said: 'Yin inherited Xia rites; what it reduced and added can be known;
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said, 'Shang inherited Xia ritual, and its additions and subtractions are intelligible;

sentences.zh: 周因於殷禮，所損益，可知也；

translations.literal: Zhou inherited Yin rites; what it reduced and added can be known;
translations.idiomatic: Zhou inherited Shang ritual, and its modifications are likewise knowable;

sentences.zh: 其或繼周者，百世可知也。」

translations.literal: as for whoever succeeds Zhou, even after a hundred generations it can be known.'
translations.idiomatic: and whoever succeeds Zhou can be understood even a hundred generations later.'

sentences.zh: 今大漢繼周，久曠大儀，未有立禮成樂，此賈宜、仲舒、王吉、劉向之徒所為發憤而增嘆也。

translations.literal: Now Great Han succeeds Zhou, yet for long has lacked great ceremonies, with no establishment of rites and completion of music; this is why Jia Yi, Zhongshu, Wang Ji, Liu Xiang, and others were stirred to indignation and deeper lament.
translations.idiomatic: Han stands in Zhou's succession, yet has long left major ceremonial institutions incomplete; that is why thinkers like Jia Yi, Dong Zhongshu, Wang Ji, and Liu Xiang spoke with such urgency and regret.
